title: Authentic Besan Ladoo Recipe
ingredients: ▢, 2 cups 2 cups (220 grams), besan, (gram flour), ▢, 0.5 cup ½ cup (100 grams), ghee, (clarified butter), ▢, 1 cup 1 cup (200 grams), sugar, (or boora shakkar or powdered jaggery, read notes), ▢, ⅔, teaspoon teaspoon, green cardamom powder, (4 to 6 cardamoms/elaichi crushed/powdered), ▢, 3 to 4, tablespoons tablespoons, cashews, chopped
instructions: 1. Powder Sugar: 2. Add sugar and cardamom powder to a grinder jar and grind to a very fine powder. Keep aside. 3. Fry Nuts: 4. Heat a pan with 1½ tablespoons ghee and fry the cashews until lightly golden. Remove to a small bowl. 5. Roast Besan: 6. Add besan and roast on a medium flame for 5 to 6 mins, until it begins to change the color & smell slightly aromatic. Reduce the heat to lowest and add rest of the ghee. 7. Deep Roast: 8. Mix well and keep roasting on a low flame. stirring continuously to prevent burning. Besan absorbs the ghee and turns to a mass at this stage. Just continue to roast, stirring constantly, until it begins to turn deep golden in color and aromatic. 9. Finishing Stage: 10. Turn off the stove when your kitchen is filled with a aroma indicating the flour is well roasted. It also turns airy and fluffy. It took me 25 mins to reach this stage. Timing may vary depending on the kind of pan and intensity of heat used. 11. Cool Down: 12. Remove the pan quickly from the stove and stir for another 2 to 3 mins to cool down. Taste test little to make sure it is nutty and not raw. 13. Prepare the Mixture: 14. Cool down the besan completely and mix in the powdered sugar (start with lesser for low sweet) until well combined. (see pictures in the post) 15. Shape Besan Ladoo: 16. Stir in the fried cashews. 17. Pinch off portions of this to your palm and press down with your fingers to shape them round like balls. (Check video) 18. Storage: 19. Store besan ladoo in a air tight jar at room temperature and use within 3 weeks. Or Refrigerate up to 2 to 3 months. Reheat them in microwave for 15 to 20 seconds or in the oven at 80 C or 160 F for a few minutes until they become warm.

title: Baingan Bharta Recipe (Eggplant Bharta)
ingredients: ▢, 5 grams 500 grams (1.1 lbs.), eggplants, (1 large baingan), ▢, 4, medium, garlic cloves, (may omit if you don't want), ▢, 1 to 2, green chilies, (slit, adjust to taste, may omit if you don't prefer), ▢, 1, teaspoon teaspoon, oil, (mustard oil or any other), ▢, 1 1 ½, tablespoons tablespoons, oil, (mustard oil or any other), ▢, 0.5 cup ½ cup (130 grams), onions, (chopped), ▢, 0.75 cup ¾ cup (200 grams), tomato, (deseeded & chopped), ▢, 3, large, garlic cloves, (fine chopped), ▢, 0.5 to, inch inch, ginger, (fine chopped), ▢, ½, teaspoon teaspoon, Kashmiri red chilli powder, (adjust to taste), ▢, 0.25 to, teaspoon teaspoon, garam masala, (start with lower amount, read notes), ▢, ¾, teaspoon teaspoon, salt, (adjust to taste), ▢, 2, tablespoon tablespoon, coriander leaves, (fine chopped)
instructions: 1. Rinse eggplants well under running water and wipe dry with a kitchen tissue. Make 4 slits on the brinjal. If you want to use the garlic & chili – push the garlic cloves deep inside 2 slits. Also push the slit green chilies in the other 2 slits. Brush them with oil. 2. On Direct Fire 3. : With the help of a tong, place it on direct fire. You can do it either on the red hot coal or stovetop. Keep turning it to all the other sides and grill until it is done completely roasted & cooked through. It takes about 12 to 14 mins. 4. To grill in oven 5. : Line a metal tray with foil. Cut each eggplant to 2 parts and place them on the tray with the skin side on top. You can also place the tomatoes in the same tray. Broil/Grill them at 460 F or 240 C for 16 to 20 mins. 6. In air fryer 7. : Place baingan, chilies, garlic and tomatoes in the air fryer basket. Air fry at 400 F or 200 C. It takes roughly about 22 mins for the whole eggplant, 16 mins if you halve the eggplant & air fry. 8. Once done, check by inserting a fork into the eggplant. It should go in smoothly & easily meaning it is soft and cooked well. (Check step-by-step photos above) 9. Cool the eggplant and peel the skin. 10. Optionally 11. – Transfer the eggplant to a bowl and cover it for 5 mins. Once done remove the skin. 12. Mash or chop the eggplant, grilled garlic and green chilies. Keep these aside. Peel and chop the tomatoes, onions and raw garlic as well. 13. Pour oil to a hot pan. When the oil turns hot, add chopped ginger and garlic. Saute for 30 to 60 seconds until aromatic. 14. Stir in the chopped onions and saute until they turn light golden, for 6 to 7 mins. 15. Add tomatoes and salt. Cook until they become soft for 5 to 6 mins. Add red chili powder and cook for a few minutes until the raw smell from the tomatoes is gone, for 1 to 2 mins. 16. Stir in the mashed eggplants, garlic and green chilies. (You may want to save half of the green chilies for later after taste testing) 17. Mix and mash it a bit to blend well. Saute for 7 to 8 minutes on a medium heat & stir in the garam masala if using. Turn off the stove. 18. Taste test and add more salt and green chili if required. Garnish with coriander leaves. If you like squeeze some lemon juice. 19. Serve baingan bharta with roti, rice or naan.

title: Basmati Rice Recipe, How to Cook Basmati Rice
ingredients: ▢, 1, cup cup, aged basmati rice, ▢, 2, cups cups, water, reduce by cup if using new rice, ▢, ½, teaspoon teaspoon, oil, or ghee, ▢, 0.25 to, teaspoon teaspoon, salt, (optional)
instructions: 1. Add basmati rice to a bowl and rinse thrice. Drain the water. 2. Soak the rice for a minimum of 15 minutes and drain to a colander/ strainer. Shake off gently to get rid of excess water. Avoid soaking if using new harvested rice. 3. Pour water to a heavy bottom pot and add salt, ghee or oil (optional). If you want you may add whole spices like star anise, cinnamon, cloves, cardamoms and cumin seeds. 4. Bring the water to a rolling boil on a high heat and then add the drained rice. You will see the temperature falls after adding rice, so let the water come back to a rolling boil on a high heat. 5. Reduce the heat to lowest and cover the pot. Cook till all of the water is absorbed, for 10 to 11 minutes. 6. Do not stir while the rice is cooking. If you see the rice bubbling and frothing up, simply open the lid 1 to 2 times before it overflows and cover back. 7. Insert a rice paddle to the bottom of the pot to check if all of the water is absorbed. Turn off when the water is completely absorbed and the rice is cooked through. 8. Cover and let rest for 10 to 15 minutes on the hot burner (stove turned off). 9. Reduce the heat to medium or medium-high so the water does not over-flow. But it is important to keep the water boiling constantly until the rice is cooked through. 10. Place a large pot under the colander or simply place your colander in the clean kitchen sink. When the rice is cooked al dente drain to the colander. 11. Transfer the rice back to the pot and cover it immediately. Let rest on the hot burner (stove turned off) for 5 to 7 mins. 12. Your fluffy and perfectly cooked basmati rice is ready. 13. Heat ghee in a wide pot and add the drained rice (completely no water). Increase the heat to high and stir fry the rice occasionally for 2 to 3 mins. 14. Pour water and bring it to a rolling boil. Reduce the heat and cover the pot. Let cook until all of the water is absorbed. 15. You have the fulffy basmati rice under 10 minutes.

title: Bengali Cholar Dal
ingredients: 1 cup chana dal, 1 tbsp ghee, 1 tsp cumin, 1 tsp ginger, 2 tbsp coconut, 1 pinch salt
instructions: 1. Soak chana dal and cook until tender 2. Heat ghee and add cumin 3. Add ginger and coconut and saute briefly 4. Add cooked dal and salt 5. Simmer until thick and aromatic

title: Besan Burfi Recipe
ingredients: ▢, 210, grams grams, besan, (2 cup gram flour), ▢, 2, tablespoon tablespoon, semolina, (fine suji, refer notes), ▢, 120, grams grams, ghee, (½ cup + 1 ½ tbsps), ▢, ¾, teaspoon teaspoon, cardamom powder, ▢, 200, grams grams, sugar, (1 cup), ▢, ½, cup cup, water, ▢, 12, almonds, or pistachios
instructions: 1. Grease a 7 inch pan and line it with a parchment paper. You can also use a steel plate. Slice handful of almonds or pistachios and keep aside. 2. Heat ghee in a large heavy bottom pan. Add semolina and let it fry for about 50 to 60 seconds. 3. Then add besan and mix well with the ghee. Begin to roast stirring continuously on a medium heat for 3 to 4 mins. At this stage the mixture will be thick. 4. Next after 3 to 4 mins, reduce the flame/heat to lowest (as low as possible) & continue to stir. As it gets fried, the mixture will turn thinner and the ghee begins to release from the besan. 5. Continue to fry stirring constantly until besan turns deep golden color and begins to smell aromatic. After frying for 20 mins, the color of the besan changes to deep golden, meaning it is done roasting. It will also turn fluffy, light, airy and very aromatic. 6. Note: Depending on the kind of besan and ghee used, your mixture may turn runny but it is not essential. If your besan is not runny at this stage, don't panic as some times it will never turn runny. It may remain in a solid or semi solid state. 7. Remove it from the stove and place it on the counter. Continue to stir for another 3 mins as we don't want the mixture to burn. Cool little besan and taste test it, it should not taste raw and should be nutty. Set this aside to cool down slightly. 8. Add sugar and pour water to a pot. Stir well and dissolve it. Begin to cook on a medium heat. 9. Let it bubble and boil until the syrup reaches a 1 string consistency. 10. Lower the flame completely and check the consistency. Take ¼ teaspoon sugar syrup from the center of the pot and cool it. 11. Take a few drops of this in between your thumb and forefinger. When you separate the fingers, you should see a single long thread. Turn off the stove immediately. 12. Check this 2 to 3 times to ensure it is right. Check the step-by-step photos above. Cool the sugar syrup a bit to slightly hot temperature. 13. Ensure besan has cooled down to slightly hot or warm temperature but the ghee is not solidified. The syrup is slightly hot & not boiling hot. If you put your finger in the syrup, you should feel it is hot and not warm. Check the picture in the step by step photo instructions so you don't go wrong at this stage. The syrup has to be all liquid and still hot. 14. Add cardamom powder to the besan and then pour half of the sugar syrup. 15. Quickly mix both of them and then pour the rest of the syrup. Mix well. You have to be quick at this stage as the mixture will begin to thicken fast. 16. Immediately pour to the greased tray. Level it with the spatula and sprinkle chopped nuts. Press down with the spatula so the nuts stick to the besan burfi. 17. It will begin to set immediately as it cools down depending on the temperature in your home. Mine took about 10 mins to cool down and set. Cut the burfi when it is slightly warm. 18. Transfer the besan burfi to a air tight jar. Store at room temperature and use up within 2 weeks.

title: Butter Naan Recipe
ingredients: ▢, 2 cups 2 cups (250 grams), all-purpose flour, (or 1 cup wheat flour + 1 cup all-purpose flour), ▢, 0.5 cup ½ cup (120 ml), yogurt, (curd), ▢, 6 to 8, tablespoons tablespoons, luke warm milk, (¼ cup + 2 tbsps) (may need more), ▢, 2, tablespoons tablespoons, oil, or melted butter, ▢, 2, teaspoons teaspoons, sugar, ▢, 0.5 to, teaspoon teaspoon, salt, (adjust to taste), ▢, 1 1 to 1 ½, teaspoon teaspoon, garlic, (optional), ▢, ½, teaspoon teaspoon, baking powder, ▢, ¼, teaspoon teaspoon, baking soda, ▢, 5 to 6, garlic cloves, sliced, ▢, 3, tablespoons tablespoons, melted butter, ▢, ¼, cup cup, coriander leaves, finely, ▢, 1, tsp tsp, kalonji seeds, (optional)
instructions: 1. To a mixing bowl add flour, baking powder, baking soda, salt, sugar and grated garlic . (If using instant yeast in place of baking powder and baking soda add it now. To use dry active yeast, refer notes.) 2. Pour yogurt, oil and 4 tbsps luke warm milk. Begin to mix to form a smooth dough adding more warm milk as needed. You may need up to half cup milk. 3. Knead well until the dough turns soft, elastic & pliable. When you press down the dough with your finger, it should dent easily. 4. Optionally you can cover and rest the dough for 1 to 2 hours in a warm place. You can also make them instantly like I made here. (For yeast dough, rest in a warm place for 2 hours or until the dough rises & doubles, Then punch the dough one to 2 times to deflate slightly). 5. Grease your fingers and divide the dough to 6 to 8 parts depending on the size you prefer. 6. Make smooth balls and keep them covered until you finish rolling the naans. 7. Sprinkle little flour on the rolling area and place a dough ball. 8. Roll the ball to 8 to 9 inches long and 5 to 6 inches wide depending on the size of the dough ball. 9. Sprinkle kalonji seeds, sliced garlic and coriander leaves. Press down or roll them gently. 10. When you are done with 5 to 6 naans, begin to heat a griddle or pan. 11. Ensure the pan is hot enough, then transfer the naan to it. Within a minute or so bubbles will be visible on top. 12. With the help of a tong, flip it and cook either on the pan or directly over the gas fire or stove. Or place a wired rack on the stove and toast it. (check video or step by step photos). 13. When you see brown spots on the naan, remove them to a plate and brush with some butter. Ensure it is cooked through on both the sides well. 14. Smear some butter on top and serve naan right away with 15. paneer butter masala 16. , 17. dal makhani 18. , 19. butter chicken 20. or any other curry.

title: Chana Masala Recipe (Chickpea Masala)
ingredients: ▢, 1, cup cup, chana, (dried raw chickpeas) (or 3 cups soaked or 2 - 15 oz cans), ▢, 1 1 ½, cups cups, water, (to pressure cook, + more to make gravy), ▢, 2, tablespoon tablespoon, oil, or as needed, ▢, 1, small, bay leaf, (optional), ▢, 1, inch inch, cinnamon, (optional), ▢, 2, cloves cloves, (optional), ▢, 2, green cardamoms, (optional), ▢, 1 1 ½, cups cups, onions, (fine chopped, 2 large), ▢, 1, green chili, slit, ▢, 0.75 to 1, tablespoon tablespoon, ginger garlic paste, (or ½ tbsp each fine chopped), ▢, 1 1 ½, cups cups, tomatoes, (fine chopped, 3 large or 1 ½ cups tomato puree or whole peeled tomatoes), ▢, ¾, teaspoon teaspoon, salt, (+ more to adjust to taste), ▢, ¼, teaspoon teaspoon, turmeric, ▢, 1 1 ½, teaspoons teaspoons, Kashmiri red chili powder, (adjust to taste), ▢, 1, teaspoon teaspoon, garam masala, (adjust to taste), ▢, 2, teaspoon teaspoon, coriander powder, ▢, ½, teaspoon teaspoon, cumin powder, (optional), ▢, 1, teaspoon teaspoon, kasuri methi, (dried fenugreek leaves) (optional), ▢, ¼, teaspoon teaspoon, amchur, (dried mango powder) (optional), ▢, 2, tbsp tbsp, coriander leaves, (cilantro chopped finely)
instructions: 1. Rinse dried chickpeas at least thrice. Soak them in 3 ½ to 4 cups of water overnight or for at least 8 hours. 2. Drain the water and rinse them well. Pour water and pressure cook for 5 to 6 whistles on a stovetop cooker. You may also cook in an instant pot for 18 minutes on a high pressure. Let the pressure drop naturally. 3. Make sure your chickpeas are soft and tender. Squeeze a chickpea to test, it should get mashed fully. If not cook them for a little longer. 4. Heat a large pot and pour oil. When the oil turns slightly hot, add the whole spices – cinnamon, cloves, cardamoms and bay leaf. 5. When they begin to sizzle add the onions and green chili. Saute until they turn light golden. 6. Add ginger garlic paste and saute for a minute without burning. 7. Add tomatoes and salt. Let cook until mushy, pulpy and thick. 8. Stir in red chilli powder, turmeric, garam masala, coriander powder and cumin powder. Saute until the masala begins to smell good, for about 3 to 4 mins. 9. Optional – If you want a smooth curry, cool this, discard the bay leaf, green chili and cinnamon. Blend in a blender to a smooth or coarse paste to suit your liking. I prefer to blend in 2 tbsps cooked chana along with the onion tomato masala. Add that puree back to the pan. 10. Add the chana along with the chana stock or water (1¼ cups). Pour another ¾ to 1 cup water. 11. If you don't puree the onion tomato masala, you will require lesser water. 12. If using canned chickpeas, drain and rinse them before adding with 1¾ cups water. 13. Mix well. Taste test and add more salt. Cover and simmer for 15 minutes. 14. When the consistency is thick (check pics), add amchur powder (optional) and kasuri methi. 15. Garnish Chana masala with coriander leaves. Sprinkle some lemon juice if required. Serve with 16. Basmati Rice 17. , Jeera Rice, Naan or Chapati. 18. To make the recipe in Instant pot, make the onion tomato masala on saute mode. Optionally If you want you may cool and blend this. Add the soaked chickpeas with 2 cups water. Deglaze and Pressure cook on high for 35 minutes. When the pressure drops, open the lid. Cook on a saute mode for a few minutes until thick. Add kasuri methi and amchur.

title: Chicken Curry Recipe
ingredients: ▢, 0.5 kg ½ kg (1.1 lbs.), chicken, (preferably bone-in, boneless is okay), ▢, 2 to 3, tablespoons tablespoons, oil, ▢, 1 cup 1 cup (3 medium), onions, (fine chopped), ▢, 1 to 2, green chilies, (slit, omit for less spicy, Thai or Indian chili peppers or serrano peppers), ▢, 1, tablespoon tablespoon, ginger garlic paste, or inch ginger 3 cloves garlic minced, ▢, 0.5 cup ½ cup (2 medium), tomatoes, (pureed or finely chopped), ▢, ¼, cup cup, yogurt, (or 1 ½ tbsp cashew butter or 12 cashews powdered & pureed with ¼ cup water, refer notes), ▢, 0.5 to, teaspoon teaspoon, salt, (adjust as needed), ▢, 0.5 to 1, cup cup, water, (or light coconut milk), ▢, 2, tablespoons tablespoons, coriander leaves, or mint leaves finely chopped, ▢, ¼, teaspoon teaspoon, turmeric powder, ▢, 1, teaspoon teaspoon, Kashmiri red chili powder, or smoked paprika, ▢, 1, teaspoon teaspoon, garam masala, (½ tsp more if needed, adjust to taste), ▢, 1, teaspoon teaspoon, coriander powder, ▢, 1, bay leaf, or 1 sprig curry leaves, ▢, 4, cloves cloves, ▢, 2, inch inch, cinnamon piece, ▢, 3, green cardamom
instructions: 1. On a medium flame, heat oil in a deep pan. Add the whole spices – bay leaf, cinnamon, cloves & green cardamoms. 2. Stir in the chopped onions and chilies. Saute until deep golden in color, for 7 to 8 mins. 3. Add ginger garlic paste and saute till you get a nice aroma, for about a minute. 4. Then add tomatoes, turmeric and salt. Mix well and cook until the tomatoes turn soft & mushy. 5. Turn down the heat to low, Stir in the yogurt (or cashew paste), red chili powder, garam masala & coriander powder. 6. Cook on low heat, until the mixture smells good and aromatic. 7. Add chicken and coriander leaves. Fry on a medium heat till the chicken turns white or pale in color. This takes around 3 mins. 8. Cover and cook on a low heat for 3 to 4 minutes. This step is very important as the chicken absorbs all the flavors of the spices. 9. Meanwhile in a separate pot, heat up 1 cup water. Using cold water most times toughens the chicken so it is good to use hot water. 10. Pour hot water just enough (about ½ cup) to make a thick gravy. As you cook, chicken lets out plenty of moisture, depending on that add more water. Check the pictures in the post for consistency. 11. Cover and cook on a medium flame till the chicken is soft cooked and the curry/gravy turns thick. When the chicken is cooked completely it will fall off the bone easily. 12. Taste the curry & add more salt if needed. You can also add another ½ tsp garam masala at this stage if you feel it is less in flavors. 13. Lastly garnish with coriander leaves, cover and off the heat. Serve chicken curry with 14. butter naan 15. or 16. jeera rice 17. .

title: Chicken Madras Curry
ingredients: ▢, 1.1 lbs, grams grams, chicken, breasts thighs, ▢, 1, tablespoon tablespoon, ginger garlic, crushed, ▢, 2, tablespoons tablespoons, coriander leaves, (handful leaves fine chopped), ▢, 1 1 ½, tablespoon tablespoon, lemon juice, ▢, 0.5 to 1, teaspoon teaspoon, Kashmiri red chili powder, (omit for low heat), ▢, ½, teaspoon teaspoon, sea salt, ▢, 1, tablespoon tablespoon, Madras curry powder, ▢, 1, teaspoon teaspoon, garam masala, (more if needed), ▢, 3, tablespoons tablespoons, ghee, (divided or use ½ ghee & ½ oil), ▢, 1 cup 1 cup (100 grams), onions sliced, ▢, 0.5 inch ½ inch (4 grams), ginger, (peeled and sliced), ▢, 3 3 (7 ½ grams), medium, garlic cloves, sliced, ▢, 1.75 cups 1 ¾ cups (200 grams), tomatoes, sliced, ▢, 0.3333333333333333 cup ⅓ cup (35 to 50 grams), carrots, sliced, ▢, 0.3333333333333333 cup ⅓ cup (35 to 50 grams), red bell pepper, sliced, ▢, 1 1 ½, cups cups, water, (divided, 1 + ½ cup hot water), ▢, 1, tablespoon tablespoon, Madras curry powder, ▢, 0.5 to 1, teaspoon teaspoon, Kashmiri chili powder, (adjust to taste or omit for low heat), ▢, 0.75 ½ to 1 ½, tablespoon tablespoon, tamarind paste, (or chutney or 1 tsp concentrate or lemon juice, adjust to taste, read notes), ▢, 0.5 to 1, tablespoon tablespoon, sugar, or jaggery, ▢, ¼, teaspoon teaspoon, salt, (adjust to taste), ▢, ½, cup cup, coconut milk, (optional, at room temperature)
instructions: 1. Marinate chicken with lemon juice, curry powder, chili powder (omit for low heat), garam masala, ginger garlic, coriander leaves and salt. Keep aside until the sauce is ready. 2. Pour half of the oil/ghee to a pot and saute the onion, carrots, ginger & garlic for 5 mins. 3. Add tomatoes and bell peppers. Saute for 2 to 3 mins and pour 1 cup water. Cover and cook until the veggies are fully cooked through and mushy. 4. Cool down & transfer to a blender. Blend to a smooth puree. 5. Pour the rest of the ghee to the pan and add marinated chicken. Saute for 5 mins on a medium heat. Stir in the curry powder, salt and chili powder. 6. Transfer the onion tomato base curry sauce and mix well. Pour hot water & mix well. Cover and cook until the chicken is fork tender for another 5 to 8 mins. 7. Stir in lower amount of tamarind/ chutney, sugar & mix well (if using lemon, add at the time of serving). Taste test and add more salt, sugar, tamarind or garam masala to taste. It should be hot, spicy, slightly sweet and tangy. 8. If it is too hot, turn down the heat and pour the coconut milk. Bring it to a gentle boil and turn off. 9. Sprinkle coriander leaves and serve hot with rice or chapati or roti.

title: Chicken Vindaloo (Restaurant Style)
ingredients: ▢, 1 1 ½, teaspoon teaspoon, cumin, (or 1 teaspoon ground cumin), ▢, 2, inches inches, Ceylon cinnamon, (or ¼ teaspoon ground cinnamon), ▢, 6, cloves cloves, (⅓ teaspoon ground cloves), ▢, 10, black pepper corn, (or ½ to ¾ crushed black pepper), ▢, ½, teaspoon teaspoon, mustard seeds, (or ¼ teaspoon ground mustard), ▢, 1 1 ½, tablespoons tablespoons, coriander seeds, (or 1 tablespoon ground coriander), ▢, 2, green cardamoms, (optional, ¼ teaspoon ground cardamom), ▢, 20, Kashmiri red chilies, soaked in vinegar cup hot water for 10 mins, ▢, 0.29166666666666663 cup ¼ to ⅓ cup (60 to 80 ml), vinegar, (malt/goan/red wine/ apple cider), ▢, 1 10 (25 grams), garlic cloves, (or 2 tablespoons fine grated), ▢, 1.5 inch 1 ½ inch (25 grams), ginger, (or 2 tablespoons fine grated), ▢, 2 lbs, skinless bone-in chicken, (or 1 ½ lbs. boneless chicken), ▢, ½, teaspoon teaspoon, turmeric, ▢, 0.75 to 1, teaspoon teaspoon, sea salt, ▢, 3, tablespoons tablespoons, oil, (I use coconut oil), ▢, 2 cups 2 cups (250 grams), onions, (2 medium, fine chopped or processed), ▢, 2, small, tomatoes, (optional) (pureed), ▢, 1, teaspoon teaspoon, sugar, (optional), ▢, 0.25 to, cup cup, water, (adjust as required)
instructions: 1. Option 1 with whole spices 2. – Grind cumin, pepper, cinnamon, cloves, mustard, cardamoms and coriander to a fine powder. Add the garlic, ginger & soaked red chilies along with the liquid. Grind to a smooth paste. 3. Option 2 with ground spices 4. – Add all the ground spices, red chili powder, grated garlic & ginger to a bowl. Pour vinegar and water only as required to make a thick paste. 5. Marinate chicken with the prepared vindaloo paste, salt and turmeric. Cover and set aside for a minimum of 1 hour to overnight in the refrigerator. If using bone-in chicken, don’t rest longer than 2 to 3 hours. 6. Heat oil in a wide pan/skillet and add the onions. Saute on a medium heat till golden, for about 9 to 10 mins. Deglaze the pan with 2 tablespoons water (or 1 tbsp vinegar + 1 tbsp water). Cook down for 2 to 3 mins until jammy. 7. Stir in the marinated chicken and saute on a medium heat for 4 to 5 mins. Mix in the tomato puree if using and cook for another 4 mins. 8. Cover and simmer until the chicken is cooked through and the sauce is thick. If the pan dries out in between, pour ¼ to ½ cup hot water. I use about ½ cup but it may be different for you. 9. If your chicken vindaloo is runny, evaporate the excess moisture by cooking further. It thickens a bit more upon cooling. Taste test and add more salt or sugar to balance the tang. 10. Chicken Vindaloo tastes best the next day. Refrigerate for the flavors to develop & reheat before serving. Steamed rice, 11. Saffron rice 12. , poee (Goan bread) or any kind of artisan breads or flatbreads like 13. Naan 14. or 15. Tandoori roti 16. go well with this.

title: Dal Fry Recipe (Restaurant Style)
ingredients: ▢, 1, cup cup, toor dal, (split pigeon peas) (or ¾ cup toor dal + ¼ cup moong dal or masoor dal), ▢, 2 2 ½, cups cups, water, (3 cups for Instant pot, more for regular pot), ▢, 2, tablespoons tablespoons, ghee, (or oil or butter), ▢, 0.5 to, teaspoon teaspoon, cumin seeds, ▢, ½, teaspoon teaspoon, mustard seeds, (optional), ▢, ⅛, teaspoon teaspoon, hing, (asafoetida), ▢, 1, red chilli, broken, ▢, 1 to 2, green chilies, (slit or chopped) (optional), ▢, 1, medium, onion, (¼ cup chopped finely), ▢, 1, teaspoon teaspoon, ginger garlic paste, (or ½ inch ginger & 2 garlic cloves fine chopped), ▢, 1, large, tomato, (½ cup chopped or pureed), ▢, ¼, teaspoon teaspoon, turmeric, ▢, ½, teaspoon teaspoon, red chili powder, (adjust to taste), ▢, ½, teaspoon teaspoon, garam masala, (adjust to taste), ▢, 0.75 to 1, teaspoon teaspoon, salt, (adjust to taste), ▢, ½, cup cup, water, (to use for consistency), ▢, 1, teaspoon teaspoon, kasuri methi, crushed, ▢, 2, tablespoons tablespoons, coriander leaves, finely chopped, ▢, 1 to 2, tablespoons tablespoons, lemon juice, or as needed
instructions: 1. Add dal to a pot or cooker. Rinse it well a few times until water runs clear. (optional – To cut down the cook time, soak them for 30 minutes and drain the water.) 2. To cook in pressure cooker 3. : Pour 2½ cups water. Pressure cook on a medium heat for 2 to 3 whistles. When the pressure releases, open the lid and mash the dal slightly. 4. To cook in pot 5. : Add dal to a heavy bottom pot and pour 3 cups water & bring it to a boil. Simmer uncovered until soft & mushy, adding more hot water as needed. To cut down the cooking time, soak it warm water for 30 mins and cook. 6. Heat a pan with ghee, oil or butter. Add mustard seeds, cumin seeds and dried red chili. When the spices begin to splutter, add hing. 7. Next add onions and green chilies. Saute until the onions turn golden. 8. Add ginger garlic and saute until the raw smell of ginger garlic goes off. This takes about a minute. 9. Then add tomatoes and salt. Saute on a medium flame until tomatoes turn soft. 10. Add red chili powder, garam masala and turmeric. Saute for 2 mins till the masala begins to smell good & leaves the pan. 11. Add the mashed dal & mix well. Pour more water as needed to bring it to a desired consistency. (watch video) 12. Cook until the dal begins to bubble well. When dal fry reaches the desired consistency, crush kasuri methi in your palms and add it. 13. Taste test and add more salt if needed. Garnish with coriander leaves. 14. Serve dal fry with rice, roti, 15. butter naan 16. or paratha. When the temperature comes down slightly squeeze in some lemon juice. 17. Press the SAUTE button instant pot and pour ghee to the steel insert. 18. When the ghee melts, add the cumin, red chilli & mustard (optional). 19. As soon as the spices pop, add hing, green chilli & onions. 20. Saute for 2 mins and then fry ginger garlic for 30 seconds. 21. Add in tomatoes and salt. Saute until the tomatoes turn soft and mushy. 22. Stir in red chilli powder, turmeric and garam masala. Add rinsed dal and pour 3 cups water. 23. Press CANCEL button. Mix well and scrape the bottom with the spatula to release any bits of food stuck. 24. Optional – You can also place a long legged trivet and then place your rice bowl and cover. 25. Secure the Instant pot with the lid. Set the steam vent to sealing position. 26. Press the PRESSURE COOK button, high pressure and set the timer to 8 minutes. 27. When the IP beeps wait for the natural release for 10 mins. 28. Mash the dal to suit your liking. Then add kasuri methi and coriander leaves. Taste test and add more salt if needed. 29. If you like thinner consistency you can add little hot water and mix. 30. Sprinkle some lemon juice over the dal fry & serve with rice.

title: Dal Makhani Recipe (Restaurant Style)
ingredients: ▢, 1 cup 1 cup (200 grams), urad dal, (Indian black lentils/ whole black gram), ▢, 0.25 cup ¼ cup (55 grams), rajma, (red kidney beans, or sub with urad dal), ▢, 4 cups 4 cups (960 ml), water to pressure cook the dal, (more to cook in pot), ▢, 2 tablespoons 2 tablespoons (30 ml), ghee, (or use oil), ▢, 3 tablespoons 3 tablespoons (42 grams), unsalted butter, (cultured butter if you can), ▢, 0.5 cup ½ cup (65 grams), onions, (fine chopped 1 small to medium), ▢, 1 tablespoon 1 tablespoon (18 grams), ginger garlic paste, each grated, ▢, 1 cup 1 cup (220 grams), tomato puree, (bottled or 2 to 3 medium tomatoes pureed in a blender), ▢, 0.875 ¾ to 1 ½, teaspoon teaspoon, Kashmiri red chili powder, (adjust to preference, I use 1 tsp), ▢, 1, teaspoon teaspoon, garam masala, (+ ½ tsp more if you want at the finishing stage), ▢, 1, teaspoon teaspoon, sea salt, (adjust as needed), ▢, 2 cups 2 cups (480 ml), water, (to add later), ▢, 1, tablespoon tablespoon, kasuri methi, (dried fenugreek leaves, optional), ▢, 0.29166666666666663 cup ¼ to ⅓ cup (60 to 80 ml), heavy cream, (cooking cream or whipping cream, more to garnish if you want), ▢, 1, bay leaf 2 green cardamoms 1 black cardamom
instructions: 1. Add urad dal and rajma to a large bowl and rinse them rubbing very well. Drain and repeat the rinse thrice. Soak in 4 cups of water, overnight. Later drain, rinse well and drain again. 2. Pour 4 cups fresh water to the dal and pressure cook for 10 whistles on a medium heat or in the instant pot for 20 mins (high pressure). If you don’t have a cooker, cook in a heavy bottom pot until soft and tender, adding more hot water as needed. 3. Both urad dal and rajma must cook down very soft. When mashed with a spoon the dal and rajma must be soft and get mashed well easily. If not then pour another half cup hot water and continue to cook for another 3 to 4 whistles on a medium heat. 4. Heat a heavy bottom pot with ghee. Add the whole spices if using and let them sizzle for a minute. 5. Stir in the onions and saute until golden, for 7 mins. Stir in ginger garlic paste and saute for 1 minute until aromatic. 6. Pour the tomato puree and cook for 3 to 4 minutes. Stir in salt, chili powder & garam masala. Saute well until the masala smells good and turns thick, for 4 to 5 mins. 7. Mash the cooked urad dal slightly and pour it along with the stock (lentil cooked liquid). Pour 1 cup hot water & mix. 8. Cook on a low flame until the dal becomes thick, stir often in between to prevent burning the bottom. After 60 mins, the dal thickens. 9. Add another ½ to 1 cup hot boiling water and continue to cook until really thick. (This takes another 30 mins. Totally cook for about 90 mins from the time you added the cooked dal to the pot. The cook time may vary depending on your pot and stove) 10. Crush kasuri methi in your palm and add it. Also pour the cream. Stir and cook again for 6 to 8 mins until it becomes thick and creamy. Taste and add more salt or garam masala if needed. Add butter and mix well. 11. Place a small piece of foil or an aluminum foil cup or a tall steel cup on the dal makhani. Hold 1 to 2 inch piece of natural wood charcoal with a tong and burn it on direct fire until red hot, for 3 to 4 mins. Then place it on the foil/cup kept over dal. 12. Quickly pour ¼ tsp ghee on the hot coal. Immediately cover the pot and allow to smoke for 3 to 4 mins. For stronger flavor, add another ¼ tsp ghee on the coal and repeat the smoking. 13. Garnish dal makhani with more butter or cream & serve with fluffy Basmati rice or naan. 14. Heat ghee in the instant pot and add onions. Saute until they turn golden, for about 5 mins. 15. Stir in the ginger garlic paste and saute for 1 minute. 16. Add the tomato puree, red chili powder, garam masala and salt. Saute for 3 to 4 mins and add the soaked & drained lentils. 17. Pour 3 cups water and pressure cook for 30 mins. Wait until the pressure drops, for about 15 mins and open the lid. 18. Mash some of the urad dal, not rajma and press saute mode. Pour 1 cup hot water, mix and continue to cook on saute mode for 12 to 15 mins (switch bet low saute to med saute & vice versa as required) 19. (Optionally if you want you may add 1 more cup hot water & cook for 12 to 15 mins more – I found this way it tastes exactly as the stovetop version) 20. Taste test and adjust salt. Stir in the crushed kasuri methi, cream and more garam masala if you want. Add the butter and turn off.

title: Dosa Recipe with homemade Dosa Batter
ingredients: ▢, 0.5 cup ½ cup (100 grams), urad dal, (whole or split skinned black gram), ▢, 2 tablespoons 2 tablespoons (25 grams), chana dal, (Bengal gram), ▢, 1.5 cups 1 ½ cups (315 grams), raw rice, (sona masuri sub with ponni/ basmati rice, refer notes), ▢, 2 tablespoons 2 tablespoons (15 grams), poha, (flattened rice), ▢, 0.5 teaspoon ½ teaspoon (2.5 grams), methi seeds, (fenugreek seeds), ▢, 1.25 cups 1 ¼ cups (300 ml), cold filtered water, (+ ¼ cup more as needed, divided - ¾ to 1 cup + ½ cup), ▢, ½, teaspoon teaspoon, sea salt, (non iodized salt if using before fermentation)
instructions: 1. Rinse together urad dal, chana dal and methi seeds thrice, draining out the water after each rinse. Soak in 3 cups water for 5 to 6 hours. 2. Rinse rice thrice and soak in 3 cups water for 5 to 6 hours. (If you have a high speed blender you may rinse and soak them together, read faqs) 3. Rinse and soak poha in ¼ cup water for 30 mins, before blending the batter. 4. Drain water from dal & transfer to a blender jar. Also add soaked poha and ¾ to 1 cup water (start with lesser). Grind to a smooth & bubbly batter. Transfer this to a large bowl or an instant pot steel insert. 5. Drain water from rice & add it to the jar. Pour ½ cup water and blend to a smooth or slightly coarse batter. 6. Transfer to the dal batter bowl and mix well. (Check video to see the final consistency). During winters you may add sea salt & mix at this stage to help with fermentation. 7. Cover the bowl loosely and ferment the batter in a warm place overnight, up to 12 hours until it rises, turns light & bubbly. 8. During colder season, ferment the batter in oven at 100 F/ 40 C for 3 hours & leave it overnight there with the oven light ON. Alternately, place the bowl in the IP or the instant pot steel insert, press yogurt settings with a timer set to 8 hours (12 to 14 hours during colder days). Use an external lid and not the IP lid. 9. Optional Fermentation test: Well fermented dosa batter rises & increases in volume. It looks airy, with plenty of tiny bubbles. To test, drop half a spoon of this into a bowl of water. Well fermented batter floats. If it has not reached this stage, ferment longer. 10. Stir the dosa batter once. Transfer the required portion to a small bowl and add salt as required. Refrigerate the rest for up to 2 weeks. 11. The fermented batter usually becomes thick, pour little water to thin down only needed & bring it to a spreadable consistency. (Check video or photos in the post) 12. Heat a dosa tawa on a medium heat. When it is slightly hot, add a few drops of oil and spread it with a kitchen paper or a half cut onion (pierced with a fork/ butter knife on the rounded side to hold).This prevents the dosas from sticking to the pan. 13. To check if the pan is hot enough, sprinkle a few drops of water over the pan. If it is ready, it should sizzle. 14. Reduce the heat to medium, stir the batter with a ladle and take a ladle (¼ cup) of dosa batter & pour in the center of the hot tawa. 15. Immediately begin to spread it evenly with the base of the ladle – starting from the center, in an outward circular motion in a clockwise direction. (Check photos or video in the post) 16. Increase the heat to medium high or high and drizzle oil/ghee/ butter across the edges. 17. Cook until the base turns golden & crisp. Run a thin wooden turner/spatula across the base of the dosa, starting from the edges to the center. 18. Optional – If you want you may cook on the other side – turn it to the other side and cook for a minute. Turn again and cook the base for 30 seconds to crisp it. 19. Fold the dosa and remove from pan. Before making the next dosa, reduce the heat to low. You can also rub the cut onion and then pour the batter. 20. Serve dosa with coconut chutney, potato masala & tiffin sambar.

title: Egg Curry Recipe
ingredients: ▢, 4 to 5, boiled eggs, ▢, 2, tablespoons tablespoons, oil, ▢, 1, teaspoon teaspoon, ghee, or oil, ▢, 1 1 ¼, cups cups, onions, or 2 medium onions diced boiled pureed, ▢, 1, cup cup, tomatoes, finely chopped, ▢, 1, green chilli, or slit, ▢, 1, teaspoon teaspoon, ginger garlic paste, or finely minced, ▢, ½, teaspoon teaspoon, salt, (adjust to taste), ▢, 1 1 to 1 ¼, cup cup, water, (adjust slightly as needed), ▢, 1, tablespoons tablespoons, coriander leaves, finely, ▢, ¼, teaspoon teaspoon, Kasuri methi, (dried fenugreek leaves, optional), ▢, ½, teaspoon teaspoon, sugar, (optional), ▢, 4, tablespoons tablespoons, cream, or 10 cashews blended with cup water, ▢, 1, small, bay leaf, ▢, 1, small, cinnamon piece, ▢, 2, green cardamoms, ▢, 0.5 to 1, teaspoon teaspoon, red chilli powder, ▢, 0.5 to 1, teaspoon teaspoon, garam masala, (refer notes), ▢, 0.5 to 1, teaspoon teaspoon, coriander powder, (optional), ▢, ⅛, teaspoon teaspoon, turmeric
instructions: 1. Fine chop or puree the onions. If you prefer to puree the onions, first boil diced onions in 2 cups of water for 4 mins until transparent. Drain the water and puree them in a blender. 2. Refer notes below to boil eggs perfectly. Wipe dry the boiled eggs or air dry until moist free. Prick each egg with a fork 2 to 3 times. We do this so the eggs don't splatter oil and burst in the pan while frying. 3. Heat 1 teaspoon ghee or oil in a nonstick pan & Fry the eggs until golden & slightly blistered. Remove the eggs to a plate & optionally sprinkle 2 to 3 pinches each of chilli powder & garam masala. 4. Heat 2 tbsp oil and add bay leaf, cardamoms and cinnamon. 5. When they begin to sizzle, add onions & green chilli. Saute onions until golden and the raw smell has almost gone. 6. Add ginger garlic paste and saute until the raw smell goes off, for about a minute. 7. Then add tomatoes and saute well until the mixture turns mushy. 8. Next add red chili powder, garam masala, coriander powder, turmeric and salt. Saute everything well until a nice aroma comes out & the mixture leaves the sides of the pan. 9. Pour water 1 to 1¼ cups water and mix well. Cover and cook until the curry thickens and you see traces of oil over the curry. 10. Taste test and adjust salt. If required add sugar. Add the fried eggs and kasuri methi. Mix and simmer for a while. Sprinkle chopped coriander leaves. Serve punjabi egg curry with roti or naan or plain rice. 11. Optional 12. – Pour 3 to 4 tbsps cream or Blend together 10 cashews with half cup water to smooth milk. Pour it to the curry. Cook covered for 2 to 3 mins until the curry turns thick. 13. Press saute button and add oil to the inner pot of the Instant pot. Add spices & bay leaf. 14. Add ginger garlic and fry for 30 seconds. Then add the tomatoes and salt. 15. Fry until the tomatoes breakdown. Add chilli powder and turmeric. Saute for a min. 16. When the cumin begins to sizzle add onions and green chilli. Saute until the onions turn light golden. 17. Then pour 1 cup water and mix well to release any bits of food stuck at the bottom of the pot. 18. Place a long leg trivet. Place your basmati rice bowl. Cover it. Then place the eggs in another bowl. Cover it. 19. Secure the IP with the lid. Position the steam release handle to sealing. 20. Press the pressure cook button (high pressure). Set the timer to 7 mins. 21. When the IP is done, press the cancel button. 22. When the pin drops, open the lid. Do a quick manual release by moving the steam release handle from sealing to venting. You can also wait for 5 mins for a natural release. 23. Pour cold water to the eggs and let them cool down. Peel the eggs and prick them with a fork. 24. Press the saute button and simmer the eggs in the curry for a minute. Then pour the cream or cashew milk or coconut milk. 25. Stir well and cook until the curry thickens. Taste and add more salt if needed. Turn off and garnish with coriander leaves.

title: Fish Curry Recipe (Fish Masala)
ingredients: ▢, 1.1 lbs, Fish, (any firm fish), ▢, ½, tbsp tbsp, ginger garlic paste, ▢, ⅛, tsp tsp, turmeric, ▢, 0.25 to, tsp tsp, Kashmiri red chili powder, ▢, ¼, tsp tsp, salt, or as needed, ▢, 1, tbsp tbsp, oil, (adjust as needed), ▢, ½, tsp tsp, cumin seeds, (jeera), ▢, 2, medium, onions, sliced about 1 cup sliced, ▢, ½, tbsp tbsp, ginger garlic, minced, ▢, 2, medium, tomatoes, ripe, ▢, ½, tsp tsp, salt, (adjust to taste), ▢, 1, tsp tsp, Kashmiri red chilli powder, (adjust as needed), ▢, ⅛, tsp tsp, turmeric, ▢, ½, tsp tsp, fennel seeds, (optional) (saunf), ▢, 2, tbsp tbsp, coconut, (grated or ¼ cup coconut milk or 12 cashew nuts), ▢, 2, tbsp tbsp, oil, (+1 tbsp to fry the fish), ▢, 1, sprig sprig, curry leaves, (pat dry or use a small bay leaf), ▢, 1 to 2, green chili, (chopped or slit), ▢, 1 1 to 1 ½, tsp tsp, garam masala, or curry powder as needed, ▢, 1.25 to 1, cup cup, water
instructions: 1. Marinate fish with ½ tbsp ginger garlic paste, ⅛ tsp turmeric, ¼ tsp red chilli powder & ¼ tsp salt. 2. On a flat fry pan, heat 1 tablespoon oil. Fry fish pieces slightly until the raw smell goes away just for 2 to 3 mins on both sides. You can also sear the fish. Keep this aside. (refer picture below) 3. Heat 1 tbsp oil in a pot and add cumin. When they sputter, add onions and saute until lightly golden. 4. Next add ginger garlic paste and saute for 30 seconds. Add tomatoes & ½ tsp salt. Saute until tomatoes turn soft and mushy. 5. Next add coconut, fennel seeds (optional), red chili powder & turmeric. Fry until the mixture turns fragrant & raw smell has gone away. 6. Cool this & add to a blender jar along with 2 to 4 tbsps water. Blend to a smooth or coarse paste to suit your liking. (refer notes). You can also use a immersion blender. Keep this aside. 7. Add 1 tbsp more oil to the same pot used earlier. Heat up on a low flame. 8. When the oil turns slightly hot, add curry leaves & green chilies. 9. When they splutter, add the ground onion tomato paste and garam masala. Taste test the paste and add more chilli powder at this stage if you want more heat or more deeper color. 10. Saute until the mixture smells good. 11. Pour 1 ¼ to 1½ cup water. Mix and bring it to a boil. Cook until you see traces of oil on the surface and the gravy thickens a bit. 12. Gently slide the fish pieces and cook covered on a medium heat. 13. Carefully flip or turn the pieces to the other side after 3 to 4 mins. 14. Cook until the fish pieces bulge a bit. This is the indication they are cooked completely. Taste the curry and add more salt if needed. 15. Optionally garnish fish curry with coriander leaves if desired.

title: Ghee Rice Recipe
ingredients: ▢, 1, cups cups, basmati rice, ▢, 2, tablespoons tablespoons, ghee, or clarified butter, ▢, 1, medium, onion, thinly sliced, ▢, 1 1 ¾, cups cups, water for Indian cooker, (1 ¼ cup Instant pot, 2 cups for normal pot), ▢, 1, green chilies, (slit or chopped), ▢, ½, teaspoon teaspoon, Salt, (adjust to taste), ▢, 14, cashewnuts, (kaju, 28 split), ▢, 14, raisins, ▢, 2, inch inch, cinnamon, (dalchini), ▢, 4, cloves cloves, (laung), ▢, 3 to 4, green cardamoms, (elaichi), ▢, 1, strand strand, mace, (javitri), ▢, 1, star anise, (chakri phool), ▢, 1, bay leaf, (tej patta)
instructions: 1. Add rice to a bowl and rinse well at least thrice. Soak it for at least 20 to 30 minutes. Drain and set aside. While the rice soaks, slice onion thinly & slit green chilies. 2. Heat a pressure cooker or a pot with ghee. Fry cashews till lightly golden, add raisins and stir up. When they turn plump set them aside in a plate. 3. To the same pan, add onions. You can also skip onions from the recipe. Fry them till golden, about to caramelize but not burnt. Remove them to a plate and keep aside to garnish. (These are the fried onions / barista. ) 4. Add all the whole spices to the pan & fry for a min until aromatic. 5. Add green chilies, drained rice & fry on a medium flame for about 3 to 4 minutes. Stir to prevent burning . 6. Pour water and salt as needed. You may need to add more or less water depending on the rice (read my notes below). 7. Taste the water and check the salt. If needed add more. Ideally the water has to be slightly salty. 8. If making in a pressure cooker, cook on a medium heat until you hear 1 whistle. If making in pot, cover and cook on a very low flame until the rice is cooked fully but not mushy. 9. When the pressure releases, Open the lid and gently fluff up the rice with a fork. 10. Transfer ghee rice to a serving bowl or plate. Garnish with raisins, cashews, fried onions and coriander leaves. 11. Serve ghee rice with any gravy like kurma, curry or even with egg roast. You can also eat it plain. 12. Press the SAUTE button & add ghee to the inner steel pot of the Instant pot. 13. When the ghee melts, add the cashews & saute until they turn light golden. 14. Then add in the raisins and stir until they plum up. Remove the cashews and raisins to a small plate. 15. Stir in the onion slices to the ghee and spread them in a single layer. 16. Next stir them every now and then until they turn golden to light brown & aromatic. If the pot is too hot, you can switch the saute to low by pressing (saute button again). Drain the ghee well and remove the fried onions to the same plate. 17. Now add in all the whole spices, followed by the green chilli and drained rice. 18. Saute for 30 to 60 seconds & press CANCEL button. 19. Pour water and add salt. Deglaze by scrubbing the bottom of the steel pot with a spatula. Taste the water and add more salt if needed. 20. Secure the instant pot with the lid. Position the steam release handle/ vent to sealing. 21. Press the PRESSURE COOK button (or manual on older IPs) and set the timer to 5 mins. 22. When the IP beeps, wait for natural pressure release for 2 to 3 mins. For softer rice, you can wait upto 5 mins. Then release the rest manually by moving the steam release handle from sealing to venting. 23. Cool down a bit and then garnish ghee rice with fried onions, raisins and cashews.

title: Green Moong Dal (Green Gram Dal)
ingredients: ▢, 0.5 cup ½ cup (105 grams), green gram, (dried whole green moong, mung beans), ▢, 0.5 cup ½ cup (60 grams), onions, chopped, ▢, 0.75 cup ¾ cup (125 grams), tomatoes, deseeded chopped, ▢, 1, green chili, chopped, ▢, ¾, teaspoon teaspoon, ginger, minced, ▢, 2 to 3, garlic, cloves minced, ▢, 1, sprig sprig, curry leaves, or bay leaf, ▢, 1.5 to 2, tablespoons tablespoons, oil, or ghee, ▢, ½, teaspoon teaspoon, cumin seeds, (jeera), ▢, ¼, teaspoon teaspoon, mustard seeds, (optional), ▢, 0.5 to, teaspoon teaspoon, Kashmiri red chili powder, (adjust to taste), ▢, ½, teaspoon teaspoon, garam masala, ▢, ¾, teaspoon teaspoon, coriander powder, ▢, ¼, teaspoon teaspoon, turmeric, ▢, ¾, teaspoon teaspoon, salt, (adjust to taste), ▢, 1.5 cups 1 ½ cups (360 ml), water, (3 cups for pot) (1 ½ cups for Instant pot), ▢, 2, tablespoon tablespoon, coriander leaves, to garnish
instructions: 1. Rinse and soak green gram for 3 to 4 hours. Avoid over soaking as the skin begins to separate on its own while cooking. Once soaked, discard the water and rinse them well. 2. Heat a pan or pot or pressure cooker with oil. Add mustard seeds (optional) & cumin seeds. 3. When they begins to splutter, fry curry leaves & green chilies for 1 minute. 4. Add onions & saute until golden. Add the ginger garlic & fry for a minute. 5. Next add tomatoes & saute until they break down and turn mushy 6. Stir in salt, turmeric, red chili powder, garam masala & coriander powder. Saute for about 1 to 2 minutes. 7. Add the green gram and pour water. 8. If cooking in a pot: 9. Cook till the green gram is tender & soft. Add more hot water if required, as you cook. 10. If using a pressure cooker 11. , cook on a medium heat for 3 whistles. When the pressure releases, open the lid. 12. If the curry is runny, mash the green moong dal a bit and cook further for a while until it becomes thick. 13. Pour a teaspoon of ghee before serving. Squeeze in some lemon juice. Garnish with coriander leaves. Serve with rice or chapati. 14. Rinse the green moong dal well and keep immersed in fresh water until you prepare the ingredients. Press saute button on your Instant pot and wait for the display to show “HOT”. 15. Pour oil or ghee. Add cumin seeds, curry leaves, onions and green chili. 16. Saute until the onions turn light golden. Stir in the ginger garlic & saute for 30 to 60 seconds. 17. Add tomatoes and sprinkle salt. Saute until the tomatoes turn soft and mushy. 18. Add chilli powder, garam masala, coriander powder & turmeric. Saute for 30 seconds & press cancel button. 19. Pour water and mix well at the bottom of the steel insert to deglaze any bits of food stuck. At this stage if you desire you can also blend the onion tomato masala using a hand blender. 20. Drain water from the green gram and add them. (Optional for cooking rice – Place a trivet and then the rice bowl. Cover the rice bowl.) 21. Secure the IP with the lid and position the steam release handle to sealing. 22. Press pressure cook button and set the timer to 12 mins. If using dried mung beans (unsoaked) then cook them for 15 mins. 23. Let the pressure release naturally for 10 mins, then release the rest manually. 24. Garnish green gram dal with coriander leaves. You can also stir in some coconut milk or lemon juice if you want.

title: Gulab Jamun Recipe
ingredients: ▢, 1, cup cup, milk powder, ▢, 5, tbsp tbsp, all-purpose flour, (maida), ▢, 1, tsp tsp, ghee, or oil, ▢, 1, tbsp tbsp, ghee, or oil for greasing, ▢, 2 to 4, tbsp tbsp, milk, (use more as needed), ▢, 1, tbsp tbsp, curd, (yogurt or ¾ tbsp lemon juice), ▢, 1, pinch, Baking soda, or 1 8 th tsp, ▢, Ghee, or oil for deep frying, ▢, 1, tsp tsp, pistachios, chopped, ▢, 1.25 to 1, cups cups, Sugar, ▢, 1 1 ½, cup cup, water, ▢, 4, pods, green cardamom, or tsp cardamom powder, ▢, 1, tsp tsp, rose water
instructions: 1. Mix together water, sugar and crushed cardamoms in a pot. 2. Boil the syrup until it turns slightly sticky. 3. Turn off the stove before it goes to a 1 string consistency. 4. If it reaches a 1 string consistency, then add 2 tbsp of water and mix. 5. Add rose water and mix. Set aside to keep it hot. 6. Fluff up the flour in the jar with a fork and then measure correctly. 7. Mix together flour, milk powder and soda in a bowl. Either sieve it or mix uniformly. 8. Add ghee to it. Mix well. 9. In a small bowl, mix together lemon juice or yogurt and 2 tbsp milk. 10. Pour 1.5 tbsp of this to the flour mixture. 11. Begin to bring the flour together to make a dough. 12. If the dough is too dry, add little more milk & curd until you get a smooth dough. Do not add a lot. Use only as needed. 13. The dough turns sticky. Grease your fingers and make a ball. 14. The dough must not be soggy. It must hold the shape well and should be smooth without any cracks. 15. Divide to 14 to 18 equal sized portions. Grease your hands and roll to smooth balls. 16. They must be smooth without any cracks or lines otherwise you will find many cracks on the gulab jamuns. Keep them covered. 17. Heat up the pan with ghee or oil on a medium heat. 18. Also check if the syrup is hot. If it is not hot, heat it up a bit. It must be hot and not very hot. 19. To check the right temp, you can drop a small piece of dough in the oil. 20. It must rise slowly without changing its color. 21. If it rises rapidly then you will need to cool the oil a bit before frying. 22. When the ghee is just medium hot, add the balls. 23. Make sure the oil is not very hot else the gulab jamuns will brown without cooking well. 24. Fry the balls on a medium flame for 1 to 2 mins. Then regulate the flame to low flame & fry until golden. Keep stirring gently to fry them uniformly. 25. Add them to the hot syrup. Allow them to rest for 3 hours and serve. 26. Garnish gulab jamun with chopped pistachios.

title: Hyderabadi Tomato Dal
ingredients: 1 cup toor dal, 2 tomatoes, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chilli powder, 1 tbsp oil, 1 tsp mustard seeds, 1 pinch salt
instructions: 1. Wash dal and pressure cook with turmeric until soft 2. Mash the dal and add chopped tomatoes 3. Simmer until the tomatoes soften 4. Heat oil and splutter mustard seeds 5. Add the tempering to dal and simmer for five minutes

title: Idli Recipe (Idli Batter Recipe with Pro Tips)
ingredients: ▢, ½, cup cup, urad dal, or skinned black gram, ▢, 1, cup cup, idli rice, or, ▢, 2, tablespoon tablespoon, thick poha, Or tsp fenugreek seeds, ▢, ½, teaspoon teaspoon, Non-iodized salt, (rock salt or pink salt), ▢, ½, cup cup, chilled water, (2 to 4 tbsps more if required, to grind dal), ▢, ½, cup cup, water, (to grind rice), ▢, ½, cup cup, urad dal, ▢, 1 1 ½, cups cups, idli rice, (or parboiled rice), ▢, ½, teaspoon teaspoon, fenugreek seeds, (methi seeds), ▢, 2, tablespoons tablespoons, thick poha, (flattened rice) (substitute with ¼ tsp methi), ▢, ½, cup cup, chilled water, (2 to 4 tbsps more if required, to grind dal), ▢, ¾, cup cup, water, (to grind rice)
instructions: 1. Add urad dal & methi seeds to a bowl. Add rice or idli rava to another bowl. 2. Wash them very well separately until water runs clear. Soak them separately in lot of water for at least 6 hrs. 3. Rinse and soak poha with ¼ cup water for about 30 mins before blending. 4. After 6 hours, drain the water from both the bowls. 5. Add dal, methi, soaked poha, salt & water to a blender jar or a wet grinder. 6. Blend to a thick, smooth, bubbly & frothy batter. The idli batter should not turn hot or even warm as it makes dense idli. If the batter is too thick you may add 2 to 4 tbsps more water while blending. 7. If you live in a hot climate then skip adding salt now and add it just before making idli else the batter turns sour. You will have to experiment with salt to know what works well for you. 8. Transfer urad dal batter to a large bowl. 9. A. If using rice : 10. Add rice to the blender with water (mentioned in the ingredients). Grind to a little coarse batter (semolina texture). Pour this to the dal batter. Mix both of them well with your clean hands. The batter must be thick yet of pouring consistency. 11. B. If using idli rava 12. : Drain off the water thoroughly from the rava. Squeeze excess water from the rava with the help of both your palms. Rava must not look soggy. It must absolutely have no water in it, else the batter will become runny. 13. Transfer this to the urad dal batter and mix well. 14. Now mix everything well. Use your hand to mix as it helps to ferment faster and better. If needed can add little water, if the batter is too thick. 15. Set the batter aside in a warm place for at least 8 to 14 hrs. The batter ferments and rises well to double the quantity. Sometimes it may take up to 18 hours depending on the weather and temperature. 16. If you live in a cold country, keep it in the oven with the light bulb ON. Or preheat the oven to 120 F or 50 C for 10 to 12 mins. Keep the loosely covered batter bowl inside & turn off. If you have a instant pot or microwave (with yogurt function), then you can also turn it ON with the yogurt setting (low) for 6 to 12 hours or until the batter doubles. 17. A well fermented batter will yield good soft idli. The batter must rise and look fluffy but not turn sour. You will need to experiment to know the fermentation time. 18. On a high flame, bring water to a boil in an idly steamer or a pressure cooker. 19. If using instant pot 20. , pour 1½ cups water to your inner pot. Press the saute button and bring the water to a rolling boil. 21. Meanwhile grease your idly plates lightly. Mix the idli batter gently 1 to 2 times only. Do not over do as the aerated batter will turn flat. Fill the molds with batter. 22. When the water begins to bubble and steam up, place the stand in the steamer. Cover and steam for exactly 10 mins on a high flame. 23. If using Instant pot 24. , when the water begins to boil, press CANCEL button. Keep the idli stand in the IP. Cover with the lid & position the steam release handle to venting . Then press STEAM button. Use an external timer to monitor & steam for 10 to 12 mins. 25. Once done turn off and wait for 2 mins. Remove the idly stand. Set aside to cool down for 2 to 3 mins. When cooled, the idli must not be wet on top. 26. With the help of a spoon remove the idlis to a plate. Serve idli with a chutney or sambar.

title: Jalebi recipe
ingredients: ▢, 1, cup cup, all-purpose flour, 125 grams, ▢, 2, tablespoons tablespoons, corn starch white corn flour, (or besan if fermenting batter) (16 grams), ▢, 1 8, teaspoon teaspoon, turmeric, or use natural food color, ▢, ½, cup cup, curd, (plain yogurt) (or water if fermenting batter) (120 ml), ▢, ½, cup cup, water, (more if needed) (120 ml), ▢, ½, teaspoon teaspoon, baking soda, (or 1 large pinch for fermented batter), ▢, 1, teaspoon teaspoon, lemon juice, ▢, oil, as needed, ▢, 1, cup cup, sugar, (use organic) (200 grams), ▢, 0.5 to, cup cup, water, (I used ½ + 2 tbsps) (120 ml to 180 ml), ▢, 1, pinch, saffron, or kesar optional, ▢, ¼, teaspoon teaspoon, cardamom powder, or elaichi powder, ▢, 1, teaspoon teaspoon, lemon juice, ▢, 1, sauce bottle, or Zip lock bag
instructions: 1. Add sugar and water to a pot. 2. Boil on a medium heat until it reaches a 1 string consistency. 3. Take a small portion of the syrup in a spoon. Cool it slightly. 4. Take it in between your thumb and fore finger. 5. Gently move the fingers away from each other, you must see a single string. 6. Pour lemon juice, cardamom powder and saffron. Remove from heat. Stir and set aside. 7. If your syrup goes beyond this stage, then sprinkle some water and mix. Recheck for the 1 string consistency. 8. A 9. . 10. Method 1: For Instant jalebi 11. : Add maida, cornflour and turmeric to a mixing bowl. Mix everything well until uniform. Next add curd. Pour water & make a thick lump free batter. The batter has to be thick but of flowing consistency. If needed add more water. Beat the batter well with a whisk in one direction in a circular motion for 4 mins. The batter will turn smooth. 12. B. 13. Method 2: For fermentation : 14. Mix together maida, besan & turmeric in a bowl. Pour water & make a thick batter of pouring consistency. Cover and ferment for 12 to 24 hours until the batter gets a very mild sour flavor. The batter will not rise, but will have tiny bubbles on top. 15. Heat ghee or oil on a medium heat to fry jalebis. 16. If using oil, then add 1 to 2 tbsp ghee to the oil. This enhances the flavor. 17. Pour 1 tsp lemon juice to the batter & mix. 18. Skip the lemon juice if you have fermented the batter. 19. Add soda and mix gently just until combined. 20. Check batter consistency: 21. The prepared batter must be smooth free flowing and thick. Spoon just 2 to 3 tbsp of batter to the sauce bottle to check if the consistency is right. 22. Next check if the oil is hot enough by dropping a small portion of the batter. It has to come up immediately without browning. 23. Now squeeze the bottle gently and move in circular motion to get spiral. If you are getting very thick jalebi, then the batter is thick. Next if you are getting very thin flat jalebis the batter is thin. 24. To fix thick batter, add a tbsp or more water. Next to fix thin batter, add a tbsp of maida (all purpose flour). 25. Take a look at the pic in the FAQ section to fix the batter. 26. Mix the batter well. Spoon it to the bottle. 27. Ensure oil is hot and the flame set to medium high heat. 28. Squeeze in the batter gently in circular motions starting from the center moving outside. 29. You can also do it the other way. You will get properly shaped ones after making a few. 30. While the jalebi is getting fried, check the syrup. It must be slightly hot to warm when the jalebi is dipped into it. If not heat up a bit. 31. The last 1 minute, turn the flame to low and fry the jalebi. This helps to make them extra crisp. 32. When the jalebi is done it turns crisp. Remove it with a skewer and add to the warm sugar syrup directly. 33. Allow to rest for 2 mins. Remove to a plate. Continue to make more jalebis. 34. Serve jalebi hot.

title: Jeera Rice Recipe (Cumin Rice)
ingredients: ▢, 1 1 ½, cups cups, aged basmati rice, (refer notes), ▢, 2, tablespoon tablespoon, ghee, or oil, ▢, 1, green chili, slit and deseeded, ▢, 2 2 ½, cups cups, water to pressure cook, or or, ▢, 0.5 to, teaspoon teaspoon, salt, as needed, ▢, 2, teaspoons teaspoons, cumin seeds, (jeera), ▢, 1, bay leaf, (tej patta), ▢, 4, green cardamoms, (elaichi), ▢, 2, inch inch, cinnamon piece, (dalchini), ▢, 4, cloves cloves, (laung), ▢, 1, strand strand, mace, (javitri) (optional)
instructions: 1. Rinse rice thrice and soak it in 3 cups water for 20 mins. Later drain to a colander. 2. On a medium flame, heat ghee in a pot or pressure cooker. Add all the whole spices and let them sizzle for 2 mins, until aromatic. 3. Stir in the green chili and rice. Fry on a high heat for 2 to 3 mins, without stirring vigorously. 4. Pour water and add salt. Mix well and taste test the water to adjust salt. Bring to a rolling boil on a high heat. 5. Option 1 – Jeera rice in a pot 6. – Reduce the heat to low and cook covered until water is absorbed & the rice is cooked through. (takes me about 15 to 17 mins) Turn off and rest for 10 mins. 7. Option 2 – Jeera rice in stovetop pressure cooker 8. – Cover the pressure cooker with a lid. Cook on a high heat until you hear 1 whistle. For al dente rice, release the pressure carefully after 2 mins with the help of a wooden spatula. When the pressure drops completely, open the lid. 9. Fluff up jeera rice with a fork before serving with curry or dal. 10. Press saute button and pour ghee to the inner pot of instant pot. 11. Add all the spices & saute for 1 min until they turn fragrant. Add the drained rice and chilli. Saute for 2 mins stirring occasionally. 12. Pour 1¾ cups plus 2 tbsps water and add salt. Stir and taste the water, to adjust salt. 13. Secure the lid & position the steam release handle to sealing. Press the pressure cook button, set to high pressure. Set the timer for 5 mins. 14. When done the instant pot beeps. Let the pressure release naturally for a min and release the rest manually with the help of a long spoon (moving the vent from sealing to venting). 15. Fluff up the Jeera rice with a fork & add some coriander leaves if you want. Serve with dal or your favorite curry.

title: Kaju Katli Recipe (Kaju Barfi)
ingredients: ▢, 1 1 ½, cups cups, cashew nuts, (kaju - 200 grams), ▢, 90, ml ml, water, (6 tablespoons, refer notes), ▢, ¾, cup cup, sugar, - 150 grams for low sweet refer notes, ▢, 1, teaspoon teaspoon, rose water, or teaspoon cardamom powder, ▢, 2, teaspoons teaspoons, ghee, for cooking, ▢, 1, teaspoon teaspoon, ghee, for greasing
instructions: 1. If you store your cashews in the refrigerator, bring them to room temperature. (Spread on a tray and leave on the counter for about 2 to 3 hours.) Add them to a grinder or food processor jar and pulse a few times until it turns to a powder. 2. Do not run the grinder for long time as the cashews begin to release oil and turn lumpy. 3. To ensure the powder is not very grainy, sieve it if you want and blend the coarse particles again. The cashew powder will be slightly grainy. 4. Add ¾ cup sugar and 90 ml water to a non stick or a heavy bottom steel pan. Heat & dissolve it well. 5. When the sugar melts completely and begins to boil rapidly, add the cashew powder. Lower the heat. 6. Mix well to blend the sugar syrup and cashew powder. The mixture must be smooth without any lumps. 7. Keep stirring and cook on a medium heat until slightly thick, for about 5 mins. Pour 1 teaspoon rosewater or ⅛ teaspoon cardamom powder and one teaspoon ghee. 8. Cook till the kaju katli mixture turns thick, for another 4 mins. It should begin to leave the sides of the pan. If it doesn't then drizzle few drops of ghee again and mix. (It takes me 9 mins in total) 9. At this stage the mixture should not look dry/crumbly but should be moist and sticky. 10. To check if the mixture is ready. 11. Turn off the stove and set the pan aside. Take ¼ teaspoon of this kaju katli mixture and cool down a bit. 12. Roll it to a ball with greased fingers. You must get a ball that isn't sticky. Upon cooling completely this has to get the texture of the katli or fudge. 13. Transfer the cooked kaju katli mixture immediately to a greased parchment paper. Let it cool down to a warm or slightly hot temperature that you can handle. 14. Grease your fingers and knead the mixture until it looks really smooth. Takes me under 2 mins and avoid over-kneading. (Alternately, hold the edges of the parchment paper to cover the mixture partially and knead it) 15. Troubleshooting 16. – If your kaju katli mixture tends to stick a lot, it means it is still undercooked. Add back to the pan and cook the mixture for a little longer. If your mixture looks dry & crumbly, sprinkle some boiled and cooled water and knead. 17. Shape the mixture to a ball and place on parchment paper. Cover it and using a rolling pin, roll to a large sheet of ¼ inch thickness. If you want place the edible silver/ vark directly on it and press down. 18. Cool completely and cut to desired shapes – squares or diamonds. Store kaju katli in an airtight box.

title: Kheer Recipe
ingredients: ▢, 4 cups 4 cups (1 liter), whole milk full fat, (for instant pot 2 ½ cups milk) (refer notes), ▢, ¼, cup cup, water, (optional or use 1 tsp ghee to grease the pot, prevents burning), ▢, 0.25 cup ¼ cup (50 grams), rice, (basmati or any rice, or use ⅓ cup to cut down the cook time), ▢, 5 tablespoons 4 to 6 tablespoons (50 to 65 grams), sugar, (4 tbsps for moderate sweetness), ▢, ½, teaspoon teaspoon, cardamom powder, (or about 2 to 3 pods powdered), ▢, ¼, cup cup, unsalted nuts, - cashews pistachios - roughly chopped, ▢, 2, tablespoons tablespoons, sweet raisins, (optional), ▢, 1, tablespoon tablespoon, ghee, optional to fry nuts raisins, ▢, 1, pinch, saffron, or 1 tsp edible rose water
instructions: 1. Wash rice a few times & drain completely. 2. Pour water (if using) and milk to a heavy bottom pot or Dutch oven. Bring it to a boil on a medium heat and stir in the rice. 3. Continue to cook on a medium to low heat, until the rice is soft, completely cooked and lightly mushy. It takes about 25 to 30 mins. During this step, keep stirring every 3 minutes to prevent the milk getting scorched at the bottom. 4. Stir in the sugar and continue to cook until it turns thicker but is yet of flowy consistency. It takes about 10 mins. (Note: becomes thicker upon cooling down) 5. Taste test, if you want add more sugar & simmer for another 5 mins (if you add more). Stir in cardamom powder, saffron (optional) rose water and chopped nuts. 6. Optional: If you prefer ghee in your rice kheer, heat ghee in a small pan and fry the nuts until light golden. Stir in the raisins & fry until plump. Pour that over the kheer. 7. Press the saute button. Optional – add ghee to the inner pot of the Instant pot. Fry the cashews until light golden. Add raisins and fry for 30 seconds. Remove to a plate. 8. Pour water (to avoid burn) and milk. Stir in rice and sugar. Secure the lid of the instant pot. Position the steam release handle to sealing. Press the porridge button. 9. Set the timer to 18 mins. When it is done press cancel & switch off. Wait for the pressure to release naturally. 10. When the float valve drops, open the lid and stir in cardamom powder & rose water. 11. Rice kheer will be runny at this stage. For a thicker consistency cook on saute mode for a few mins until thick. It will thicken further after cooling. 12. Transfer kheer to serving bowls & garnish with nuts & raisins. You can also chill the rice kheer and serve.

title: Khichdi Recipe
ingredients: ▢, 1 to 2, tablespoons tablespoons, ghee, or oil, ▢, 0.75 to 1, teaspoon teaspoon, cumin seeds, ▢, 1, bay leaf, or 1 sprig of curry leaves, ▢, 0.75 to 1, teaspoon teaspoon, ginger, or ginger paste, ▢, ¼, cup cup, onion, finely chopped, ▢, 1, green chili, slit, ▢, ½, cup cup, tomatoes, deseeded fine chopped, ▢, ¼, teaspoon teaspoon, turmeric, ▢, ⅓, teaspoon teaspoon, salt, (adjust to taste), ▢, ½, cup cup, rice, (any kind or quinoa, steel cut oats, millets), ▢, ½, cup cup, moong dal, (skinless petite yellow lentils) (or any other dal), ▢, 4, cups cups, water, (more to adjust, for porridge consistency), ▢, 1, pinch, asafoetida, (hing) (optional), ▢, ½, tablespoon tablespoon, ghee, for topping, ▢, 1, medium, carrot, cubed, ▢, 8, french beans, cut to 1 inch length, ▢, 1, small, potato, cubed
instructions: 1. Add rice and moong dal to a pot. Rinse them at least thrice & drain water completely. If you do not have a cooker, then soak them for 30 to 40 mins. This will help them cook faster. 2. Heat ghee in a pressure cooker or pot on a medium heat. Once the ghee is hot, add bay leaf (optional) and cumin seeds. 3. Soon the seeds begin to splutter, then add ginger & saute until fragrant, for about 30 to 60 seconds & sprinkle hing. 4. Add onions and green chilies. Saute them until the raw smell of onions goes away & they begin to turn golden. 5. Next add tomatoes, carrots, beans, salt and turmeric. Saute for 3 to 4 mins. 6. Then add drained daal and rice. Saute for 3 to 4 mins. 7. Pour 4 cups water for porridge like consistency or 3 cups for mushy consistency. 8. Stir well & taste the water. Add more salt if needed. 9. Cover the pressure cook and let it cook for 2 whistles on a medium flame for a mushy khichdi. Cook for 4 to 5 whistles on a medium flame for porridge consistency. 10. Let the pressure release on its own, then open the lid and mix well. If the consistency is too thick for your liking, pour about ½ to ¾ cup hot boiling water and simmer for 1 to 2 mins until dal khichdi begins to bubble. Taste test and adjust salt. 11. If cooking in a pot, bring water to a boil on a high flame. Then reduce the flame to medium to low and continue to cook. 12. Leave the pot uncovered or partially covered so the froth won't overflow. Keep checking and add more hot boiling water (about ½ to ¾ cups) as needed until the lentils are fully soft cooked. 13. When both are done, mash the khichdi slightly so you get a thicker consistency. Adjust salt as needed. 14. Transfer to serving plates and pour 1 tsp ghee on the hot khichdi. Serve with papad and pickle. 15. Press saute button (medium) on your Instant pot. Pour ghee to the steel insert. 16. As soon as the ghee melts, add bay leaf and cumin seeds. 17. When cumin begins to sizzle add onions & chopped chili. 18. Saute until onions turn transparent. Next add in ginger, hing, tomatoes, all veggies & turmeric. 19. Saute for 2 to 3 mins until tomatoes turn mushy. 20. Add drained rice & lentils. Saute them for another 2 to 3 mins. 21. Next add salt & pour water. Deglaze the pot by scraping the bottom with a spatula to remove bits of food stuck in there if any. Mix well & taste the water. 22. Add more salt if needed. Secure the lid & move the steam release handle to sealing. 23. Press the pressure cook or manual button (high pressure). Set the timer to 8 mins. Or you can also press porridge button & set timer to 9 mins. 24. Once it is done, the instant pot beeps, then press cancel and switch off. 25. Wait for a natural pressure release for 10 minutes & release the rest manually. 26. When the float valve drops, open the lid & stir gently. 27. If you like soupy khichdi, add some hot boiling water to bring to consistency you like. Saute for 1 minute if you prefer. 28. Top dal khichdi with little ghee & serve with pickle or papad or chutney.

title: Lamb Curry Recipe
ingredients: ▢, 2.2 lbs, bone-in lamb, (shoulder or leg, bone-in or bone-less cut to 1 ½ inch), ▢, ¼, cup cup, yogurt, (for substitutes read notes), ▢, 2 to 3, teaspoons teaspoons, Kashmiri red chilli powder, (adjust to taste, read notes), ▢, ½, teaspoon teaspoon, turmeric, ▢, 2 to 3, teaspoons teaspoons, garam masala, (or meat masala, adjust to taste, read notes), ▢, 0.75 to 1, teaspoon teaspoon, cumin powder, ▢, 1, tablespoon tablespoon, coriander powder, ▢, 2, teaspoon teaspoon, salt, (more to adjust), ▢, 6, tablespoons tablespoons, oil, or ghee, ▢, 1, bay leaf, ▢, 6, green cardamoms, ▢, 3, inch inch, cinnamon piece, ▢, 2, black cardamom, (optional), ▢, 1 to 2, star anise, (optional), ▢, 2, cups cups, onions, chopped, ▢, 1.5 cup 1 ½ cup (300 g), tomatoes, (fresh tomatoes chopped or pureed or 1 cup bottled like passata), ▢, 1 to 2, green chilli slit, ( deseeded, optional), ▢, 2, tablespoon tablespoon, ginger garlic paste, (or minced, divided equally), ▢, 4, tablespoons tablespoons, mint leaves, (pudina, fine chopped), ▢, 4 to 5, cup cup, water, (cut down for boneless, adjust as needed), ▢, ½, tablespoon tablespoon, kasuri methi, (dried fenugreeks leaves, optional), ▢, ¼, cup cup, coriander leaves, (fine chopped, optional)
instructions: 1. Add all the ground spices to a small bowl and mix them. 2. Marinate lamb with half of this spice mix, half of the ginger garlic, salt, kasuri methi and yogurt (or lemon juice/ vinegar). Cover and set aside for about 2 hours to overnight in the refrigerator. 3. Heat 1 to 2 tablespoon oil/ghee in a heavy bottom pan and add star anise, bay leaf, black cardamom, green cardamom & cinnamon. 4. Once the spices begin to sizzle, add the mint leaves and stir in the marinated lamb and braise on a medium heat for about 8 to 10 minutes. 5. Pour half of the hot water and mix well. Cover and let cook on a low to medium heat for 1 hour. Keep stirring every 15 mins and add more hot water as you cook. 6. Meanwhile in another pan, heat the rest of the oil/ghee. Add the onions & green chilies. Saute on a medium heat for 6 to 7 mins. 7. Reduce the heat and stir fry for a few more minutes until deep golden but not burnt. 8. Add rest of the ginger garlic & saute until the raw smell of ginger garlic goes away. This takes 1 to 2 minutes. 9. Turn off the heat & stir in the rest of the ground spices, immediately pour the tomato puree. Cook on a medium heat until the raw flavor of tomatoes is gone. 10. Transfer this to the lamb after 1 hour of cooking lamb. Cook covered on a low heat until the meat is fully cooked and fork tender, for another 50 mins or more. Pour more hot water if required as you cook & keep stirring often, every 5 to 7 mins. 11. Taste test and add more salt or garam masala & kasuri methi if needed. If you want more heat in your lamb curry, add some freshly crushed black pepper. 12. Garnish lamb curry with coriander leaves and serve with roti/ naan/ rice or with any artisan bread. 13. Lamb curry tastes best after resting for a few hours or even the next day. Reheat & adjust the salt when you are ready to serve.

title: Masala Chai Recipe (Indian Masala Tea)
ingredients: ▢, 4, green cardamoms, (elaichi or ¼ teaspoon ground), ▢, 2, cloves cloves, (3 for stronger flavor or ⅛ teaspoon ground), ▢, 0.5 to, inch inch, cinnamon piece, (cassia or Ceylon or ⅛ teaspoon ground cinnamon), ▢, 2, pepper corn, (or a pinch of ground pepper, optional), ▢, 1 1 ½, cups cups, water, ▢, 1, cup cup, full fat milk, (adjust to taste, Refer notes), ▢, 2.5 to 3, teaspoons teaspoons, black tea leaves, or 1 to 1 teaspoon tea powder, ▢, 2 to 3, teaspoons teaspoons, sugar, or as needed, ▢, 0.5 teaspoon ½ teaspoon (½ inch), ginger, or crushed, ▢, 1, teaspoon teaspoon, green cardamoms, (5 grams skinned) (elaichi), ▢, ½, teaspoon teaspoon, cloves cloves, (2 grams), ▢, 2.5 to 3, grams grams, cinnamon, (cassia or Ceylon), ▢, ¼, teaspoon teaspoon, fennel seeds, (1 ½ grams) (saunf), ▢, 0.25 to, teaspoon teaspoon, black pepper corn, (½ tsp ground pepper), ▢, ½, teaspoon teaspoon, nutmeg, or of large nutmeg, ▢, 2, petals, star flower, (star anise) (chakri phool)
instructions: 1. Add cloves, cardamom, cinnamon stick & black pepper to a mortar pestle or a spice grinder. Crush or grind them to a powder. 2. Heat water in a pot. Add the ground spices, crushed/chopped ginger and tea. (Start with lower amount of tea and if required you may add more after adding milk) 3. Bring this to a rolling boil and reduce the heat to medium. Boil for 3 mins until the decoction turns dark. 4. Pour milk and add sugar. Bring it to a boil on a medium high heat. (If using tea bags remove them at this stage and keep aside.) 5. Reduce the heat and simmer until the chai (tea) becomes dark, for about 2 mins. 6. When the masala tea is ready, you will see a layer of cream on top and also the milk flavor is gone. 7. Optionally aerate or pull your masala chai for the same Chaiwallah taste 8. . Take a soup ladle and aerate the masala chai or simply pour some chai to a measuring cup. Pour the hot tea in a slow stream, back to the chai pot from a height of 1 to 1½ feet. 9. Repeat this step of pulling hot masala chai from the pot to the cup, a few times (4 to 5 times). This breaks down the layer of cream and incorporates it back to the masala chai making it more tasty and thick. 10. Strain the masala chai to serving cups & serve with any breakfast, snack or biscuits. 11. Clean all the spices and add to a blender jar. Powder finely. Store it in an airtight glass jar. 12. Use about ½ to ¾ tsp or more to make 2 servings of tea. For stronger tea you can use about ¾ tsp.

title: Masala Pasta Recipe (Indian Style Pasta)
