ingredients: ▢, 1.5 cups 1 ½ cups (120 grams, 4 oz), Penne Pasta, (or any other of choice), ▢, 2 2 to 2 ½, tablespoon tablespoon, olive oil, or, ▢, ½, teaspoon teaspoon, cumin seeds, (jeera), ▢, 2 to 3, cloves cloves, garlic, (grated, minced or paste), ▢, ½, inch inch, ginger, (optional - peeled & grated, minced or paste), ▢, 0.5 to, teaspoon teaspoon, salt, (+ 1 tsp for boiling pasta), ▢, ½, teaspoon teaspoon, Kashmiri red chili powder, (adjust to taste, use up to 1 ¼ tsp), ▢, ⅛, teaspoon teaspoon, turmeric, (optional), ▢, ½, teaspoon teaspoon, garam masala, (or pav bhaji masala/ meat masala/ kitchen king masala), ▢, 1, tablespoon tablespoon, kasuri methi, (dried fenugreek leaves or 1 teaspoon Italian herbs), ▢, 1 1 to 1 ½, tablespoon tablespoon, tomato ketchup, (optional or 2 tbsps passata or 1 teaspoon paste), ▢, 2 to 3, tablespoon tablespoon, cream, or cup grated cheese, ▢, 1 to 2, tablespoons tablespoons, coriander leaves, chopped, ▢, ½, cup cup, onions, (1 medium onion chopped finely), ▢, 1.25 cup 1 ¼ cup (120 grams), tomatoes, do not use sour tomatoes, ▢, ½, cup cup, carrots, chopped, ▢, ¼, cup cup, green peas, (frozen or boiled, optional), ▢, ¼, cup cup, capsicum, (bell pepper, chopped, optional), ▢, 2 to 4, tablespoons tablespoons, sweet corn kernels, or 3 baby corn sliced
instructions: 1. In a large pot, bring 6 cups water to a rolling boil. Add one teaspoon salt, a few drops of oil (optional) and pasta. 2. Cook till al dente on a medium flame. I suggest referring the instructions on the pack of pasta for the correct timings. It takes 8 to 9 mins to cook penne. 3. While the pasta cooks, rinse and chop the veggies. 4. When the pasta is cooked to al dente, reserve ¾ cup pasta cooked water in a cup and drain the pasta off to a colander. Optionally if you want you may rinse them under running water. 5. Heat oil & butter (optional) in a pan. When the oil turns hot, add cumin seeds & let them splutter. 6. Stir in the garlic and ginger & saute for a minute. 7. Add onions and sauté them on a medium high heat until they turn golden. 8. Add chopped veggies and fry for about 2 to 3 minutes. Stir in the ground spices & salt. 9. Time to add the tomatoes & the ketchup/ passata (optional). Mix well and cook on a medium heat until the tomatoes cook down and the raw flavor of tomatoes is gone. You will also see oil begins to separate from the masala & the veggies are cooked through. Else cover & cook on a low heat. 10. Pour ¼ to ½ cup pasta cooked water or plain water. Mix well & cook for a few minutes, until the sauce turns thick & saucy. (Do not over cook) 11. Stir in kasuri methi & cooked pasta. Taste test & adjust salt. Cover and cook on a medium heat for 2 to 3 mins, until the pasta turns hot & steaming. 12. If you want you may stir in heavy cream or grated cheese. 13. Garnish masala pasta with coriander leaves. Serve hot.

title: Masoor Dal Recipe (Red Lentil Dal)
ingredients: ▢, 0.5 cup ½ cup (95 grams), masoor dal, (red lentils), ▢, 2 tablespoons 2 tablespoons (23 grams), moong dal, (optional), ▢, 2 medium, tomatoes, (deseeded & chopped), ▢, 1, green chili, (optional), ▢, 1.25 to 1, cup cup, water, (or 1 cup for IP + ¼ cup if using moong dal), ▢, 1 to 2, tablespoon tablespoon, ghee, or, ▢, 1, red chili, broken, ▢, 2, garlic, cloves chopped, ▢, ¼, teaspoon teaspoon, mustard seeds, (optional), ▢, ½, teaspoon teaspoon, cumin seeds, ▢, 1, pinch, asafoetida, (hing), ▢, ½, teaspoon teaspoon, salt, (adjust to taste), ▢, 0.25 to, teaspoon teaspoon, Kashmiri red chili powder, (use up to ¾ for heat, adjust to taste), ▢, ¼, teaspoon teaspoon, turmeric, ▢, ⅛, teaspoon teaspoon, garam masala, (more if you want), ▢, ½, teaspoon teaspoon, kasuri methi, (dried fenugreek leaves), ▢, 2, tablespoons tablespoons, coriander leaves, to garnish, ▢, 1 to 2, tablespoons tablespoons, lemon juice, to serve
instructions: 1. Add masoor dal & moong dal (optional) to a pot or pressure cooker. Wash it a few times until the water runs clear. 2. Next add chopped tomatoes, water and green chili. 3. Pressure cook for 1 to 2 whistles on a medium heat. If cooking in a pot, bring the water to a rolling boil, reduce the heat and cook until soft, adding more hot water if needed. Skim off any froth that rises to the top. 4. Once the pressure drops, open the cooker. 5. Mash the dal to suit your liking. You can make it smooth or keep it coarse for a texture. If needed you can pour 2 to 3 tbsp hot water to bring it to a consistency. 6. Heat ghee (or oil or butter) in a small pan, on a medium heat. 7. Add mustard seeds, cumin seeds, dried red chilies and chopped garlic. 8. Saute until the garlic turns slightly golden. Turn off the heat and add hing, chili powder, turmeric & garam masala. 9. Quickly pour this off to the dal. (If you want you may set aside half of the tempering along with red chili to garnish later.) 10. Time to add salt and crushed kasuri methi if using. Mix and simmer for 2 mins. Taste test and add more salt if required. 11. Squeeze in some lemon & garnish with chopped coriander leaves before serving. Masoor dal is ready to serve with roti or rice. 12. Press saute button on the Instant pot and pour oil to the inner pot. 13. Add mustard, cumin, broken red chilies and garlic. 14. When they splutter add tomatoes along with salt, chilli powder, hing, garam masala (optional) and turmeric. 15. Saute for 1 min. Next add dal, green chilli and pour water. 16. Stir well and deglaze to release any bits of food stuck. Secure the Instant pot with lid. Set the steam release handle to sealing. 17. Optional 18. :You can cook basmati rice together by placing the rice bowl on a long legged trivet. 19. Press pressure cook button and set the timer for 7 mins. 20. Wait for the pressure to release naturally. When the pin drops, open the IP. 21. Add kasuri methi and stir well. Taste test and add more salt or garam masala if required. If the dal is runny, cook on saute mode for a few minutes. If it is too thick pour little hot water and saute for a minute. 22. Squeeze lemon juice and garnish with chopped coriander leaves. Serve masoor dal with rice.

title: Moong Dal Recipe
ingredients: ▢, ½, cup cup, moong dal, (yellow mung lentils), ▢, 0.25 cup ¼ cup, onions shallots, (optional, 1 small fine chopped), ▢, 0.5 cup ½ cup (1 medium), tomatoes, (deseeded & chopped), ▢, 1, tablespoon tablespoon, oil, or ghee, ▢, ½, teaspoon teaspoon, cumin seeds, ▢, 1, teaspoon teaspoon, ginger, (peeled & fine chopped), ▢, 1, green chili, (slit or chopped, deseeded if required), ▢, 4 to 5, curry leaves, (or 1 bay leaf), ▢, ⅛, teaspoon teaspoon, turmeric, ▢, ¼, teaspoon teaspoon, Kashmiri red chilli powder, ▢, ½, teaspoon teaspoon, garam masala, ▢, 1 1 ¾, cups cups, water, ▢, 1, teaspoon teaspoon, kasuri methi, (dried fenugreek leaves, optional), ▢, ½, teaspoon teaspoon, amchur, (optional, dried mango powder or 1 tbsp lemon juice), ▢, 1, tablespoon tablespoon, ghee, or oil, ▢, 2 to 3, garlic cloves, crushed, ▢, 1, red chilli, ▢, ¼, teaspoon teaspoon, mustard seeds, (optional), ▢, ½, teaspoon teaspoon, cumin seeds, ▢, 6 to 8, curry leaves, (optional), ▢, ⅛, teaspoon teaspoon, hing, (asafoetida, optional), ▢, ¼, teaspoon teaspoon, red chilli powder
instructions: 1. Add moong dal to a bowl and rinse them thrice. Soak in 1¾ to 2 cups water until you chop and prepare the rest. (If you don't have a cooker, soak them in hot boiling water for 30 mins before you proceed with the below steps) 2. Heat oil in a cooker or pot and add cumin seeds. When the seeds splutter, add ginger, green chilies and 4 to 5 curry leaves. Fry until aromatic for about a minute and add onions if using. Saute until transparent for 2 to 3 mins. 3. Add tomatoes and salt. Saute for 1 to 2 mins. Stir in turmeric, red chilli powder and garam masala. To bring out the flavors, optionally saute for 1 to 2 mins. 4. Add the moong dal along with the soaked water. Mix well to deglaze the bottom of the cooker. Cover and pressure cook for 4 to 5 whistles on a medium flame. 5. When the pressure drops, open the lid and add kasuri methi and amchur. If you prefer you can mash the moong dal a bit. Taste test and add more salt if required. 6. Moong dal is ready at this stage and you can serve it as is. For extra flavour you may proceed to the next steps. 7. Heat 1 tablespoon ghee in a small pan, on a medium flame. Add the crushed garlic and dried red chillies. Let the garlic fry a bit but not brown. 8. Turn down the heat to low and add mustard seeds, cumin seeds and curry leaves. Turn off the heat and stir in red chilli powder and hing. 9. Pour this over the moong dal and mix gently. When the dal cools down a bit squeeze some lemon juice and serve. 10. Pour oil or ghee to the steel insert of the Instant pot and press the saute button. When the oil turns slightly hot, add cumin seeds & curry leaves or bay leaf, followed by ginger, green chilies and onions. 11. Saute onions for 1 to 2 mins. Stir in turmeric, red chilli powder & garam masala. Press Cancel. Add tomatoes, moong dal & pour water. Deglaze the pot well. 12. Optionally if you want, you may place a long legged trivet and place your rice bowl over that. You will use only short grain rice here. The cook time for basmati won’t match. 13. Secure the IP with the lid and position the steam release valve to sealing. Press the pressure cook button and set the timer to 10 minutes. 14. Let pressure drop naturally. Open the lid, add kasuri methi & give a good stir. Taste test and adjust salt. If the consistency is too thick, you may pour some boiling hot water and mix. 15. Though you can serve the moong dal at this stage, since this recipe is for a moong dal tadka, make an additional tadka (next step). 16. Heat ghee in a small pan on a medium heat. Add crushed garlic cloves and red chilies. When the garlic begins to smell good, add mustard seeds, cumin seeds and curry leaves (optional). 17. Turn off the stove and stir in red chilli powder and hing (optional). Pour this all over the Instant pot moong dal. Before serving give a gentle mix and add some coriander leaves. Squeeze in some lemon juice.

title: Mutton Curry | Mutton Masala Gravy
ingredients: ▢, 0.5 kg ½ kg (1.1 lbs.), mutton, (bone-in or boneless lamb/goat, cut to 1 ½ to 2 inch), ▢, 2, tablespoons tablespoons, yogurt, (curd), ▢, ¾, tablespoon tablespoon, ginger garlic paste, ▢, ¾, teaspoon teaspoon, salt, (use as needed), ▢, ¼, teaspoon teaspoon, turmeric, ▢, 1, teaspoon teaspoon, meat masala, (or garam masala), ▢, ½, teaspoon teaspoon, Kashmiri red chili powder, (adjust to heat preference, leave out for low heat), ▢, 2, tablespoons tablespoons, oil, ▢, 3, green cardamoms, elaichi, ▢, 1, inch inch, cinnamon, ▢, 1, cup cup, onions, or processed, ▢, 0.75 cup ¾ cup (1 large), tomatoes, or chopped, ▢, 1 to 2, green chilies, slit, ▢, 2, teaspoons teaspoons, meat masala, or garam masala, ▢, 2, sprigs sprigs, curry leaves, or 1 bay leaf, ▢, ½, teaspoon teaspoon, salt, or as needed to adjust later, ▢, 1 to 2, tablespoons tablespoons, coriander leaves, cilantro chopped to garnish, ▢, ¾, cup cup, water, to cook in stovetop pressure cooker, ▢, 2 2 to 2 ½, cups cups, water, to cook in a regular pot Dutch oven
instructions: 1. Marinate mutton with salt, ginger garlic, turmeric, garam masala or meat masala, chili powder and curd. Yogurt & ginger garlic paste are natural meat tenderizers so they make the mutton succulent. 2. Set aside until you prepare the rest or leave it in the refrigerator to marinate overnight. (When you are ready to cook, leave the cold mutton out on the counter for 30 mins to bring the meat closer to room temperature.) 3. On a low to medium flame, gently heat oil in a pan or pressure cooker. Add the whole spices – cinnamon, cardamoms and bay leaf (optional), followed by onions & green chilies. 4. Saute until onions turn golden. Lower the flame to very low as much as possible. Then transfer the marinated mutton and saute for 5 to 7 mins. 5. Stir in the meat masala & curry leaves (optional), followed by pureed tomatoes. 6. Increase the heat to medium and saute for another 5 to 7 mins, until the raw flavor of tomatoes has reduced. Pour hot water & mix well. 7. To cook in a regular pot: 8. Bring it to a gentle boil and cook covered on a medium heat for 1 hour 30 minutes. Stir well every 12 to 15 minutes and pour more hot water in batches as needed. 9. Stovetop Pressure Cooker: 10. Pressure cook on a medium heat for 4 to 5 whistles, depending on the kind or cooker, meat and size of the mutton pieces. 11. When the pressure drops, open the lid and mix well. If the curry is runny, cook/saute for a few more minutes to evaporate some of the moisture. Your mutton is cooked through when it is tender, flakes easily with a fork and falls off the bone easily. 12. Taste the curry and add more salt if needed. If you feel it is low on flavor, you may sprinkle little garam masala. Garnish with fine chopped coriander leaves. 13. Serve mutton curry hot with rice, tandoori roti, naan or plain paratha.

title: Palak Paneer Recipe (Indian Spinach Paneer)
ingredients: ▢, 15 grams 150 grams (1 ¼ cups), paneer, (Indian cottage cheese), ▢, 3.5 to 4 cups, palak, (spinach), ▢, 2, tablespoons tablespoons, oil, (or half oil & half butter), ▢, 2, green chilies, (deseeded) (less spicy kind), ▢, 0.75 cup ¾ cup ( 90 grams, 1 small), onions, (fine chopped), ▢, 0.5 cup ½ cup (1 small), tomatoes, (deseeded & chopped or pureed), ▢, ¾, teaspoon teaspoon, ginger garlic paste, (read notes for substitute), ▢, ½, teaspoon teaspoon, salt, (use as per your taste), ▢, 8 to 10, cashewnuts, (read notes for substitutes), ▢, 0.5 to, teaspoon teaspoon, garam masala, (adjust to taste), ▢, ½, teaspoon teaspoon, kasuri methi, (dried fenugreek leaves) (skip if you don't have), ▢, ¼, cup cup, water, to blend spinach, ▢, ¾, cup cup, water, to cook the gravy, ▢, 3, tablespoons tablespoons, cream, (optional), ▢, ⅛, teaspoon teaspoon, cumin seeds, (jeera) (optional), ▢, 2, green cardamoms, (elaichi) (optional), ▢, 1, inch inch, cinnamon, (dalchini) (optional), ▢, 2, cloves cloves, (laung) (optional)
instructions: 1. Pluck only the young & tender spinach leaves and discard the stems as they may leave a bitter taste. If using baby spinach you can use the stems as well. 2. Add them to a large pot of water. Rinse them well few times & drain to a colander. 3. Allow the water to drain completely otherwise it will let out lot of moisture while cooking. 4. Heat half tablespoon oil in a pan. Saute green chilies, cashews and spinach for 3 to 4 mins until the leaves wilt off thoroughly & raw smell of spinach has gone away. [OR another option is to blanch the palak in 4 cups of hot water with ¼ tsp salt for 2 mins. Then immerse in ice cold water. Drain completely.] 5. Cool this completely. Blend this along with water to a smooth puree. The puree should be smooth and thick. You may add 1 to 2 tbsps more water to help in blending. 6. Heat 1 tablespoon butter and half tablespoon oil to the same pan, Once they melt, add cinnamon, cardamoms, cloves & cumin seeds. 7. When the spices begin to sizzle, add onions and fry till they turn transparent to golden. 8. Next saute ginger garlic paste for 1 to 2 minutes or until you begin to smell it nice. 9. Then add tomatoes with salt. Saute until they break down and turn mushy. 10. Add garam masala & saute until the masala smells good. This may take 2 mins. (for a smoother curry refer notes) 11. Pour water and cook covered until onions are completely soft. There should be some water left in the pan. (for consistency check video) 12. Lower the flame, add kasuri methi and pureed spinach. Mix well and cook until it begins to bubble for about 2 to 3 mins. If the curry is too thick you may add a few tbsps of hot water. 13. Avoid overcooking. Add paneer & mix well. Turn off and remove to a serving bowl. Optionally garnish with cream. 14. Serve palak paneer with 15. naan 16. , roti, 17. Basmati rice 18. or 19. Jeera rice 20. .

title: Palak Tofu (Spinach Tofu)
ingredients: ▢, 200, grams grams, extra firm pressed tofu, cut to bite size pieces, ▢, ¼, teaspoon teaspoon, salt, ▢, 1, tablespoon tablespoon, lemon juice, mixed with 2 tablespoons water, ▢, 0.25 to, teaspoon teaspoon, Kashmiri red chili powder, ▢, 0.25 to, teaspoon teaspoon, garam masala, ▢, 15, whole cashew nuts, (soak in ½ cup boiling water or use ½ cup thick coconut milk, read notes), ▢, 1 1 ½, tablespoons tablespoons, unrefined virgin coconut oil, (+ ½ tablespoon more to fry), ▢, ¾, cup cup, onions, (90 grams, 1 small), ▢, 1 to 2, green chilies, serrano peppers, ▢, ½, inch inch, ginger, peeled and chopped, ▢, 3, medium, garlic, cloves chopped, ▢, ½, cup cup, tomatoes, (90 grams, 1 small), ▢, 5, cups cups, baby spinach, (120 grams, after cleaning), ▢, ½, teaspoon teaspoon, sea salt, (adjust to taste), ▢, 0.5 to, teaspoon teaspoon, garam masala, (adjust to preference), ▢, 0.25 to, teaspoon teaspoon, toasted cumin powder, ▢, ¼, teaspoon teaspoon, cardamom, ▢, 1, teaspoon teaspoon, crushed kasuri methi, (dried fenugreek leaves), ▢, 1, tablespoon tablespoon, butter, (optional)
instructions: 1. Marinate: 2. In a medium bowl, mix together chili powder, garam masala, salt, lemon juice and water or yogurt. Add pressed tofu and gently coat the marinade. Cover and rest until needed later or in the refrigerator overnight, up to 2 days. 3. Heat oil in a pan and add onion, ginger, garlic and green chilies. Saute for 5 to 6 mins, until transparent. 4. Add tomatoes & sauté until mushy. Pour ¼ cup water. Cook covered until the tomatoes and onions become soft. 5. Stir in the spinach and saute until wilted & the raw smell is gone, for 4 to 5 mins. 6. Cool down and transfer to a blender. Add the soaked cashews with the soaking liquid or coconut milk. 7. Blend to a smooth puree, adding more water/ coconut milk to bring to a consistency (I used ¼ cup more). Return to the pan & stir in salt, cardamom, garam masala & cumin powder. Cook for 3 to 5 mins, until thick. 8. Add crushed kasuri methi and turn off. 9. Pan Fry: 10. Meanwhile, heat oil in a pan and add the marinated tofu. Pan fry until golden, turn with a spoon and fry on all sides. (Takes me about 6 to 7 mins.) Optionally add butter and mix well to coat the tofu with melted butter. 11. Air Fry or Bake 12. : If you want to air fry or oven bake, preheat the oven to 400 F or 200 C for 15 mins. Spread the tofu on a prepared baking sheet and air fry or bake for 15 mins, shaking after 9 mins. 13. Mix in 1 tablespoon lemon juice to the palak gravy. Layer the pan fried tofu over the curry. 14. Optionally garnish with coconut milk or whisked Greek yogurt. Serve Palak Tofu with flatbreads like roti, chapati, naan, bread or over rice.

title: Paneer Butter Masala Recipe
ingredients: ▢, 1 tablespoon 1 tablespoon (15 ml), oil, ▢, 2, green cardamoms, (or ¼ to ⅓ tsp ground cardamom, elaichi), ▢, 1 cup 1 cup (130 grams), onions, (sliced or diced from 1 medium yellow or 2 small red onions), ▢, 1 inch 1 inch (10 grams), ginger, (peeled & sliced or use 1 ½ to 2 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, read notes), ▢, 5.5 5 to 6 (12 grams), large, garlic cloves, (peeled, omit to use gg paste), ▢, 2 cups 2 cups (350 grams), tomatoes, (sliced, use up to 420 grams from 2 large or 4 medium), ▢, 15 15 (30 grams), whole cashew nuts, (use up to 18 nuts/ 36 g if using more tomatoes or use almonds), ▢, 1, teaspoon teaspoon, sea salt, (adjust to taste), ▢, 1, teaspoon teaspoon, jaggery sugar, (optional), ▢, 0.75 to 1, teaspoon teaspoon, Kashmiri red chilli powder, (or paprika) (adjust to heat & color preference), ▢, 0.75 to 1, teaspoon teaspoon, garam masala powder, (adjust to taste), ▢, 1, teaspoon teaspoon, coriander powder, (daniya powder) (optional), ▢, 1 cup 1 cup (240 ml), cold water, (to blend), ▢, 2 tablespoons 2 tablespoons (30 grams), unsalted butter, ▢, 1, small, bay leaf, (tej patta, optional), ▢, 2, inch inch, Ceylon cinnamon piece, (or 1 inch dalchini, optional), ▢, 3, cloves cloves, (laung, optional), ▢, 275 grams 250 to 300 grams (9 to 10 oz), paneer, (I cut mine to ¾ inch), ▢, ½, tablespoon tablespoon, kasuri methi, (dried fenugreek leaves, crushed), ▢, 3.5 tablespoons 3 to 4 tablespoons (45 to 60 ml), heavy cream, (sub with fresh cream, cooking cream or thickened cream), ▢, 1 to 2, tablespoon tablespoon, coriander leaves, (cilantro finely chopped, to garnish)
instructions: 1. Pour oil to a pan and add cardamoms, onions, ginger and garlic. Saute for 4 to 5 mins, until the onions turn transparent. 2. Add tomatoes, salt and cashews. Mix well and cook covered until the tomatoes turn mushy and break down. (Takes me about 7 mins on a medium heat. If your pan is dry, add a splash of water). 3. Stir in the red chili powder, coriander powder and garam masala. Saute on a medium heat for 3 to 4 mins, until aromatic. Turn off and cool down. 4. Pour 1 cup cold water to a blender jar & transfer the onion tomato masala. Blend to a silky smooth puree. 5. On a low heat, melt butter in the same pan and add the optional whole spices if using – bay leaf, cinnamon and cloves. 6. When they begin to sizzle, stir in the optional red chili powder. Hold a strainer over the pan and pour the onion tomato masala. Stir with a spoon to strain. 7. Pour ½ cup more water & mix well. Cook covered on a medium heat until slightly thick, yet flowy. It will thicken upon cooling. (Takes about 10 to 12 mins, keep stirring every 3 to 4 mins). You will see traces of fat on the butter masala when it is ready. 8. Remove the whole spices to discard and taste test to adjust salt and garam masala. Stir in the jaggery, kasuri methi & paneer. Cook covered for 2 to 3 mins on a low heat and turn off. Stir in the cream (reserve a tablespoon to garnish). 9. Garnish paneer butter masala with cream and chopped coriander leaves. Best served with Basmati Rice, Jeera Rice, Butter Naan, Tandoori Roti or plain parathas.

title: Poori Recipe, How to make Puri
ingredients: ▢, 1, cup cup, wheat flour, (atta), ▢, ¾, teaspoon teaspoon, semolina, (fine rava or chiroti rava) (optional), ▢, ¾, teaspoon teaspoon, oil, (or ghee), ▢, ¼, cup cup, water, (plus 1 to 2 tbsp) (adjust as needed), ▢, ⅛, teaspoon teaspoon, salt, (adjust to taste), ▢, oil, (for deep frying)
instructions: 1. Mix together flour, salt, rava and oil in a large mixing bowl. 2. Pour ¼ cup water. Begin to make a little stiff & tight dough, adding more water as needed. (check video) 3. Smear ½ tsp oil, knead lightly and smoothen the ball. 4. Do not knead it a lot otherwise poori will turn soft and absorb oil while frying. 5. Keep the dough covered all the time until the last poori is rolled. Do not rest the dough for more than 3 to 5 minutes. The longer it rests quicker the puffed puri will collapse. They will turn oily after frying. 6. Divide the dough to 8 to 10 portions. Avoid kneading them. 7. Smoothen all the balls by rolling them lightly in between your palms. Keep them covered in the bowl. 8. Drizzle a few drops of oil on the rolling area and spread it. 9. Smear oil over the dough ball & flatten it. 10. Begin to roll it evenly from the center to a round puri. 11. It should not be too thick or thin. It must be moderately thick which helps the puri to puff. 12. Make 5 to 6 puris and set them aside. Do not stack them. 13. Begin to heat the oil on a medium high flame and continue to make rest of the pooris. 14. The oil has to be hot enough. Check by dropping a small portion of dough to the pan. It has to rise immediately without browning. 15. If it browns quickly then reduce the flame slightly and allow the temperature to come down. 16. Slide the puri carefully to the hot oil. Do not disturb until it rises half way to the surface. 17. Quickly but gently begin to press down with a perforated ladle. This helps the poori to puff well. 18. Once puri puffs, flip it carefully. Fry on the other side as well until golden and crisp. 19. You can fry flipping too and fro once more until slightly crisp & to get the desired color – light golden to dark golden. 20. Remove the puri to a steel colander. Fry the next poori the same way. 21. Make sure the oil is hot enough, but not smoking before frying the next puri. Serve poori with potato curry, chutney or kurma.

title: Punjabi Jeera Aloo
ingredients: 4 potatoes, 1 tbsp oil, 1 tsp cumin, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp coriander powder, 1 pinch salt
instructions: 1. Boil potatoes until just tender 2. Peel and cube the potatoes 3. Heat oil and crackle cumin seeds 4. Add spices and potatoes 5. Toss until the potatoes are coated and lightly crisp

title: Rava Dosa Recipe | Crispy Suji Dosa
ingredients: ▢, ½, cup cup, rava, (fine semolina or suji), ▢, ½, cup cup, rice flour, ▢, ¼, cup cup, all-purpose flour, (maida or ½ cup oats flour or whole wheat pastry flour) (refer notes), ▢, 2 to 3, tablespoons tablespoons, onion, very fine chopped, ▢, 1, green chilli, finely, ▢, 1, teaspoon teaspoon, ginger, ▢, ½, teaspoon teaspoon, cumin seeds, (jeera), ▢, 0.25 to, teaspoon teaspoon, crushed black pepper, ▢, ½, teaspoon teaspoon, salt, (adjust to taste), ▢, 6, curry leaves, very finely, ▢, 2, tablespoons tablespoons, coriander leaves, very finely, ▢, 2 2 ½, cups cups, water, (you may need more), ▢, 1, tablespoon tablespoon, curd, (plain yogurt) (optional), ▢, 2 to 3, tablespoons tablespoons, oil, or ghee
instructions: 1. Add rava, rice flour, all-purpose flour, salt, ginger, green chilies, coriander leaves, curry leaves to a bowl. 2. Next add cumin, crushed pepper, onions and yogurt (optional). 3. Pour 2½ cups water and begin to make the batter. Add more water and bring it to a runny consistency. 4. Taste the batter and add more salt if needed. Rest the batter for 20 mins. 5. Heat dosa pan on a medium high heat. If using cast iron pan, then drizzle few drops of oil over the pan and spread it well with a sliced onion or kitchen tissue or cloth. Wipe off the excess oil & let it heat. 6. While the pan heats, stir the batter well. Ensure it is very runny and is pourable. At this stage you can add more water to bring to the desired consistency. 7. When the pan is hot enough, stir the batter very well and begin to pour it from the edges across the pan in a circular motion. 8. Then fill up the gaps if any by pouring more batter. 9. Reduce the flame to low. Pour 1 tsp oil across the edges and a little over the rava dosa. 10. When it is done cooking, it will begin to peel off the edges on its own. Do not pull the dosas forcibly. 11. If you make very very thin (paper thin dosas) then you don't need to cook on the other side. 12. When the rava dosa base becomes crisp & golden, remove it to a serving plate. 13. Optionally If the rava dosa has browned at the bottom but still moist on top, then you can also flip and toast the dosa on the other side. And back again. 14. Ensure the pan is hot again. 15. Stir the batter well before pouring the batter every time. If needed add more water to bring it to consistency. 16. It is best served hot as it turns soft when it cools down. Serve with any chutney.

title: Rava Idli Recipe
ingredients: ▢, 1, cup cup, semolina, (bombay rava/ suji/ chiroti rava), ▢, 2, tablespoon tablespoon, ghee, ▢, ½, teaspoon teaspoon, mustard seeds, ▢, ¼, teaspoon teaspoon, cumin seeds, ▢, ½, tablespoon tablespoon, chana dal, bengal gram, ▢, 1, tablespoon tablespoon, cashews, chopped, ▢, 10, curry leaves, ▢, 2, green chilies, chopped, ▢, ¾, teaspoon teaspoon, ginger, or paste, ▢, ⅓, teaspoon teaspoon, sea salt, (or use as needed), ▢, 2 to 3, tablespoon tablespoon, coriander leaves, finely, ▢, ¾, cup cup, runny curd yogurt, (or ½ cup Greek yogurt/ thick yogurt diluted with ¼ cup water), ▢, ½, cup cup, water, to soak rava, ▢, 1, teaspoon teaspoon, lemon juice, (omit if using eno), ▢, 1, teaspoon teaspoon, Eno, or tsp Soda bicarbonate, ▢, 1, teaspoon teaspoon, oil, to grease idli plates, ▢, 4, whole cashewnuts, or 8 split, ▢, 1, teaspoon teaspoon, carrot, optional
instructions: 1. Heat 2 tablespoons ghee in a pan and fry the cashews until light golden. Remove to a plate and reserve aside. 2. Add mustard, cumin, broken cashews and chana dal. Fry until the dal turns golden. Reduce the flame and add curry leaves, green chilies and ¾ teaspoon ginger. 3. Saute just for 30 seconds and stir in the rava. Mix well and roast on a low to medium heat until crunchy and aromatic (about 5 mins). Cool completely. 4. Once cooled, add salt, coriander leaves, curd and water. Mix well to form a batter and rest for 30 mins. 5. After 25 mins, grease the moulds and place a cashew in each of the moulds. Sprinkle some carrots. 6. Bring 2 cups water to a boil in a steamer or cooker. (or use 1.5 cups in IP). Meanwhile check the consistency of batter. If it is too thick sprinkle 1 to 2 tbsps water and mix. 7. The batter consistency should be thick, of dropping consistency not pouring consistency. Stir in lemon juice and soda bicarbonate. If using eno, use only eno and omit lemon juice. 8. Give a gentle mix and avoid overmixing. Divide the batter to idli moulds. (I get 8 portions in mine) 9. Place the idli stand in the hot steamer and cover. Steam for 10 mins on a high flame. A tester or a fork inserted into the center comes out clean if it is well cooked. 10. Cool down for 3 to 4 mins, demould them with a wet spoon. 11. Serve rava idli with 12. coconut chutney 13. or potato sagu (recipe in the post).

title: Red Lentil Curry
ingredients: ▢, 1 cup 1 cup (200 grams), red lentils, (split or whole), ▢, 2 2 ½, cups cups, water, (more if needed, same for IP), ▢, 2 to 3, tablespoons tablespoons, oil, ghee, ▢, 1, sprig sprig, curry leaves, or bay leaf, ▢, 3, inch inch, cinnamon piece, tsp cumin seeds, ▢, 0.875 cup ¾ to 1 cup (1 medium), onions, chopped, ▢, 0.75 tablespoon ¾ tablespoon (3 medium), garlic cloves, pressed, ▢, 0.75 tablespoon ¾ tablespoon (½ inch slice), ginger, crushed, ▢, 1, green chili, (optional, chopped or slit, serrano peppers/ Thai/ Indian green chilies), ▢, 1.75 cups 1 ¾ cups (3 medium), Tomatoes, (peeled & chopped/pureed or 14 oz. can crushed tomatoes), ▢, 1, teaspoon teaspoon, sea salt, (more to adjust, cut down if your curry powder has salt), ▢, ½, teaspoon teaspoon, Kashmiri chili powder, paprika, ▢, 1, tablespoon tablespoon, curry powder, (mild - or 2 tsp coriander powder, ¾ tsp cumin pow, ¼ tsp each turmeric, chili pow, cardamom, fennel, black pepper ), ▢, 0.75 to 1, teaspoon teaspoon, garam masala, (adjust to taste), ▢, 2, teaspoons teaspoons, kasuri methi, (dried fenugreek leaves), ▢, ⅓, cup cup, thick coconut milk heavy cream, yogurt cashew cream, ▢, 2 to 3, tablespoons tablespoons, coriander leaves, to garnish
instructions: 1. Add red lentils to a pot and rinse them thrice. Drain and pour water. Bring to a rolling boil on a high heat and reduce to medium. 2. Let cook until tender (al dente), for 10 mins. If you want you may skim the froth. Older lentils take longer to cook. While the lentils cook, chop and prepare the other ingredients. 3. Heat oil/ghee in a medium pan. Add the whole spices, onions, green chilies and curry leaves. Saute until golden, for about 8 mins. 4. Stir in ginger garlic and saute for a minute, until aromatic. 5. Stir in the curry powder, garam masala and Kashmiri chili powder, followed by tomatoes & (a splash of water if using fresh tomatoes). Cover and cook until the tomatoes break down. 6. Saute for 1 to 2 mins until the mixture turns aromatic. 7. Add the red lentils along with the leftover stock/ water. Mix well and more water as needed. You will need about ½ cup. Bring it to a boil and simmer for 2 to 3 minutes. 8. If you want mash some of the lentils with the back of the spoon. Stir in kasuri methi and coconut milk or cream. Simmer for 2 minutes. 9. Taste test. If you want, add more salt, garam masala or black pepper for heat. 10. Garnish with coriander leaves and squeeze some lemon juice. 11. Serve red lentil curry over rice or with Naan, Chapati, Roti or any other flatbreads. 12. You don't need to cook the lentils separately. Make the masala first, add the lentils and pressure cook for 7 minutes. Wait for natural pressure release and add kasuri methi and coconut milk or cream. 13. You don't need to cook the lentils separately. Make the masala first, add the lentils to the cooker. Pressure cook for 2 to 3 whistles on a medium heat. Wait for the pressure to drop and add kasuri methi and coconut milk or cream. If you want make a extra tadka.

title: Rogan Josh Recipe (Lamb Rogan Josh)
ingredients: ▢, 1.1 lbs, bone-in mutton, (tender lamb, like leg of lamb or shoulder, cut to 2 inch pieces), ▢, 2 to 3, tablespoons tablespoons, ghee, or mustard oil, ▢, ¼, teaspoon teaspoon, asafoetida, (hing, skip if you don't have), ▢, ½, cup cup, curd, (yogurt at room temperature, preferably fresh and not sour), ▢, ¾, teaspoon teaspoon, salt, (adjust to taste), ▢, 1 1 to 1 ½, tablespoon tablespoon, Kashmiri red chili powder, (or 2 tbsps. Kashmiri red chili paste), ▢, 1, cup cup, water, (or mutton stock, + more as required, up to 5 cups depending on the meat), ▢, 1 1 ¼, cup cup, sliced shallots, or yellow onion, ▢, 1, bay leaf, (skip if you don't have), ▢, 3 to 4, cloves cloves, (or ¼ teaspoon ground cloves), ▢, 1, inch inch, cinnamon, (or ¼ teaspoon ground cinnamon), ▢, 3, green cardamoms, (or ⅓ teaspoon ground cardamoms), ▢, 1, black cardamom, (skip if you don't have), ▢, 2, teaspoon teaspoon, fennel seeds powder, ▢, 1, teaspoon teaspoon, ginger powder, (or substitute 1 teaspoon ginger paste), ▢, 1, teaspoon teaspoon, garam masala, (optional)
instructions: 1. This step is optional. 2. But it helps to thicken the rogan josh. Heat 1.5 tablespoons oil in a pan and saute shallots/onions on a medium heat until golden, about to caramelize but not burnt. Cool them and blend with 2 to 3 tablespoons of yogurt to a smooth paste. Keep this aside. 3. Heat oil or ghee in a heavy bottom pot and add bay leaf, green cardamoms, cinnamon, cloves and black cardamoms. (If you opt for the ground spices, add them later.) 4. When the spices begin to sizzle in oil, add mutton and sear it for 1 to 2 minute on a high heat. 5. Reduce the heat to medium and fry for another 4 to 5 minutes until the meat is slightly brown. 6. While you fry the meat, add yogurt to a mixing bowl. Also add ground spices – red chilli powder, fennel powder, dried ginger powder and salt. 7. Whisk all of them well. If your yogurt is too thick (like Greek yogurt), add 3 to 4 tablespoons of water or stock and whisk it well until smooth. 8. Once the meat is light brown in color, add hing to the oil. Take the pot off the stove and slowly stir in the spice and yogurt mixture. 9. Mix well and place the pot back on the stove. Cook covered for 3 to 4 minutes on a medium heat. 10. Pour 1 cup hot water or stock. Cook covered on a low heat until tender, adding more hot water if required. 11. Stir rogan josh every 7 to 8 minutes. When it is done, the mutton pieces should be soft, tender and fall off the bone. ( 12. Optional 13. – During the last 20 minutes of cooking add the blended onion paste and mix well.) 14. The cook time depends on the kind of pot, source of heat and the intensity of heat. Do not forget to add more hot water (if required) as you cook. It takes me 2 hours for the mutton to get tender. 15. The gravy in Rogan josh is neither too runny nor too thick but is slightly thick as the some of the bone marrow seeps into the liquids. This results can be achieved only by slow cooking the dish. 16. Taste test and add more salt or garam masala if required. Turn off the heat and rest for few hours before serving. This helps to develop the flavors. 17. Serve rogan josh with plain steamed basmati rice, 18. Saffron Rice 19. , 20. Tandoori Roti 21. , 22. Butter Naan 23. , 24. Paratha 25. or Roti.

title: Samosa Recipe (Punjabi Potato Samosa)
ingredients: ▢, 2 cups 2 cups (250 grams), all-purpose flour, (organic/ unbleached), ▢, 0.25 cup ¼ cup (60 ml), oil, or melted ghee, ▢, 6 tablespoons 6 tablespoons (90 ml), water, ▢, ¾, teaspoon teaspoon, carom seeds, (ajwain), ▢, ¾, teaspoon teaspoon, salt, ▢, 4 medium, potatoes, ▢, ½, cup cup, green peas, (boiled or frozen), ▢, 1, tablespoon tablespoon, oil, or ghee, ▢, 1, tablespoon tablespoon, ginger, minced, ▢, 1 to 2, green chilies, chopped, ▢, 1, pinch, hing, (asafoetida) (optional), ▢, 4, tablespoons tablespoons, coriander leaves, finely, ▢, 1, teaspoon teaspoon, lemon juice, (or ½ tsp amchur or chaat masala), ▢, ½, teaspoon teaspoon, salt, (adjust to taste), ▢, ¾, teaspoon teaspoon, cumin seeds, ▢, 0.75 to 1, teaspoon teaspoon, garam masala, ▢, ¾, teaspoon teaspoon, red chilli powder, (adjust to taste), ▢, ½, teaspoon teaspoon, cumin powder, ▢, ½, teaspoon teaspoon, fennel powder, (optional), ▢, Mint Chutney, (or make from separate recipe card below), ▢, Tamarind Chutney, (or make from separate recipe card below)
instructions: 1. Peel, halve and boil potatoes in a pot of water, until fork tender (not mushy). Or (pressure cook unpeeled potatoes for 5 whistles on a medium flame or in the instant pot for 8 mins, placed over a trivet. When the pressure drops, cool & peel them.) 2. Crumble the potatoes, don't mash them. Keep aside. 3. Mix together flour, carom seeds, salt and oil in a mixing bowl. 4. Rub the flour well with your fingers or in between your palms for 3 to 4 mins to incorporate the oil. It should look like breadcrumbs and this is very important to make flaky samosa. 5. Take handful of flour and press down with your fingers. It must hold shape and not crumble. This is an indication that the oil is well incorporated. (check video or photos in the post) 6. Add water little by little and mix the flour to form a slightly stiff yet pliable dough. (Check pictures in the post). It should not be very soft like roti/naan dough. Cover and rest for 25 to 30 mins. 7. Heat a pan with oil. Add cumin seeds. 8. When they begins to sizzle, add ginger & green chilies. Fry until ginger smells aromatic, for about 30 to 60 seconds. 9. Stir in the green peas and saute for 2 mins. 10. Add red chilli powder, garam masala, cumin powder, chaat masala (optional) & fennel powder. Saute for 30 seconds. 11. Stir in the potatoes and sprinkle salt. Mix and saute for 2 to 3 mins. Mix in coriander leaves & set aside to cool. 12. Taste and add more salt if needed. If using lemon juice, add it now. Divide the mixture roughly to 10 portions. 13. Knead the dough gently to smoothen a bit. Divide it to 5 portions and roll to balls. Cover the dough balls until the end, to prevent drying out. 14. Grease the rolling area and then flatten a ball. Drizzle a few drops of oil. 15. Begin to roll to a circle or oval shaped disc. (Around 8.5 inches long by 6.5 inches wide). It should be neither too thick nor too thin. (Watch video) 16. Using a knife cut it to two semicircles. The 2 parts make 2 samosas. If the edges are too thick, gently roll it to thin down. 17. Work on one part – apply water over the straight edge (towards the cut side). Join the edges to make a cone. Press down gently to seal the cone from inside as well. (Check video or photos above) (If you have trouble sticking them, make a paste of flour and water, smear that lightly & then stick) 18. Fill the cone with one portion of potato masala and press down with your finger to push it inside the cone. Smear water generously on both the other edges. 19. Bring the edges together and seal them by pinching off the edges together. If you prefer to make a standing samosa, make a pleat on one side. Bring back the pleat and seal it by pinching off the edges together. (Check video or photos above) 20. Make sure the samosa is sealed well. Cover them with a cloth to prevent drying. 21. After you make 5 samosas, begin to heat the oil until medium hot. 22. Oil has to be medium hot and not very hot or cold. A piece of dough dropped in the oil should not sizzle or rise immediately. Instead you should see tiny bubbles in the oil & the dough should take a while to come up to the surface. This is the right temperature. 23. Gently add as many samosas as you can to the oil and fry them on a low flame, undisturbed for few minutes (about 10 to 12 mins). 24. When the crust becomes firm, increase the flame to medium. Turn them to the other side and fry until crispy and golden. 25. While the first batch of samosas fry, make the rest of them. Remove the golden fried samosas to a wired rack or a steel colander. Let the temperature of oil come down slightly before you fry the next batch. 26. Serve samosa with mint chutney, sweet tamarind chutney or tomato ketchup. 27. Preheat the oven at 180 C or 360 F for about 20 mins. Brush each samosa generously with oil all over and place them on a prepared tray. Bake these for about 35 to 40 mins. 28. Spray your samosa with oil or brush with ghee. Preheat your air fryer for 7 mins at 360 F or 180 C if required. 29. Place the samosa in the air fryer basket or tray spacing them at least an inch apart from each other. 30. Air fry for 12 mins, turn them to the other side and air fry for another 5 to 6 mins, or a little longer until golden and crisp. (In non-preheated air fryer air fry at 370 F or 190 C for the same time) 31. Brush them with ghee or oil as required.

title: Sprouted Green Moong (Gram) Stir Fry
ingredients: ▢, 1 1 to 1 ½, tablespoon tablespoon, ghee, (or extra virgin coconut oil/ olive oil), ▢, 0.25 to, teaspoon teaspoon, mustard seeds, ▢, 0.5 to, teaspoon teaspoon, cumin seeds, ▢, 1, teaspoon teaspoon, urad dal, (skinned/ omit if you don’t have), ▢, 1 to 2, red chilies, ▢, 1 to 2, sprigs curry leaves, ▢, 1 to 2, green chilies, ▢, 0.5 to, inch inch, ginger peeled minced, ▢, 3 to 4, medium, garlic cloves minced, ▢, ¼, teaspoon teaspoon, turmeric, ▢, ⅓, teaspoon teaspoon, sea salt, ▢, ⅛, teaspoon teaspoon, hing, ▢, 3 to 4, tablespoons tablespoons, water, ▢, 3 cups 3 cups (250 grams), sprouted green moong, ▢, ¼, cup cup, raw coconut, ▢, 2, tablespoon tablespoon, coriander leaves chopped, 2 tbsps crushed roasted sesame seeds, ▢, 0.75 to 1, tablespoon tablespoon, lemon juice
instructions: 1. Give the sprouts a quick rinse with fresh clean water. Drain completely. 2. On a medium flame, heat a pan with ghee or oil. Add mustard seeds, cumin seeds, urad dal and dried red chilies. 3. When the urad dal turns light golden, add curry leaves, green chilies, ginger and garlic. 4. Saute for 1 to 2 mins, until ginger garlic begins to smell aromatic. Stir in turmeric, salt, hing and pour water. 5. Let the water come to a rolling boil and turn down the heat to the lowest. 6. Stir in the sprouts & cover. Pan-steam for 3 to 4 mins, until the sprouts are partially cooked, yet crunchy. 7. If the pan dries out and they are still undercooked, add a splash of water and continue to steam for another 1 to 2 mins. 8. Turn off and stir in the coconut. When you are ready to serve, stir in the lemon juice, sesame seeds and coriander leaves (optional). 9. Serve stir-fried sprouted moong on their own or with roti, parathas or plain steamed rice.

title: Tandoori Chicken Recipe (Smoky Restaurant Style)
ingredients: ▢, 0.75 kg ¾ kg (1.5 lbs.), chicken, skinless thighs, ▢, ½, cup cup, Greek yogurt, (, hung curd, ), ▢, 1, tablespoon tablespoon, ginger garlic paste, (or ¾ tbsp each, grated), ▢, 1, teaspoon teaspoon, garam masala, (more if you prefer), ▢, 1 to 2, teaspoon teaspoon, Kashmiri red chili powder, (or paprika, adjust to heat preference), ▢, ¼, teaspoon teaspoon, black pepper crushed, (powder), ▢, 1 1 ½, teaspoon teaspoon, coriander powder, (daniya powder), ▢, ½, teaspoon teaspoon, cumin powder, (jeera powder), ▢, ½, teaspoon teaspoon, sea salt, (+ more to adjust or use ¼ tsp each sea salt & black salt / kala namak), ▢, ¼, teaspoon teaspoon, turmeric, (haldi, optional), ▢, 1, teaspoon teaspoon, kasuri methi, (dried fenugreek leaves), ▢, 1, tablespoon tablespoon, lemon juice, (or 1 teaspoon amchur/ dried mango powder or use ½ teaspoon chaat masala), ▢, 1 1 ½, tablespoon tablespoon, oil, (any or neutral oils like avocado/grapeseed/ refined coconut or use mustard oil), ▢, 1, tablespoon tablespoon, coriander leaves, to garnish, ▢, 1, teaspoon teaspoon, Kashmiri red chili powder, ▢, 1 to 2, tablespoon tablespoon, oil, (any or neutral oil or use mustard oil)
instructions: 1. In a large mixing bowl, stir together yogurt, ginger garlic paste, garam masala, red chili powder, salt, turmeric, kasuri methi, pepper, coriander powder, cumin powder, oil and lemon juice or chaat masala. Taste test, adjust salt and spice/heat levels to your taste. 2. Pat dry chicken with paper towels and make deep slits (3 to 4 on larger thighs or 2 to 3 on smaller). Add them to the marinade & coat them well. 3. Cover and rest in the refrigerator for least 6 hours to overnight. 4. Preheat the oven to 470 F – 240 C. (If using a smaller oven like a OTG, preheat at 430 F – 220 C). Place a wire rack over a baking tray, lined with foil for easy cleanup. Spray oil over the wire rack. 5. Arrange the marinated chicken on the rack. Roast/bake the chicken for a total of 25 to 30 mins (depending on the size of chicken), basting with leftover marinade or with chili oil/ melted butter, after the first 12 to 15 mins. (Check at 12 mins mark) 6. Turn the chicken to the other side after basting and roast for another 12 to 15 minutes. Move the tray to the top rack and broil for 3 minutes to 4 mins, to get some charred spots. The timings can vary depending on your oven and the size of chicken. So adjust accordingly. 7. When the chicken is cooked through, an instant-read thermometer inserted into the thickest part should read 165 to 170 F (75 C). Optionally you may smoke the chicken following the steps in my post. 8. Brush the drippings or melted butter all over the chicken to keep it moist. Garnish with chopped coriander leaves. 9. Serve with this 10. Cilantro Chutney 11. or Pudina 12. Mint Chutney 13. alongside onion and lemon wedges. For a complete meal, serve it with 14. Turmeric rice, 15. kachumber and Cucumber raita.

title: Tomato Chutney (Restaurant Style)
ingredients: ▢, 2 cups 2 cups (330 grams), tomatoes, (chopped from 3 medium tomatoes), ▢, 0.75 cup ¾ cup (110 grams), onions, (sliced/diced from 1 medium onion, red/yellow), ▢, 3.5 3 to 4 (7 grams), large, garlic cloves, (peeled), ▢, 0.5 inch ½ inch (5 grams), ginger, (peeled & sliced), ▢, 1, sprig sprig, curry leaves, (or sub with 10 mint leaves or 2 to 3 tender stalks of coriander leaves), ▢, 1.5 tablespoon 1 ½ tablespoon (22 ml), oil, ▢, 0.5 tablespoon ½ tablespoon (6 grams), chana dal, (Bengal gram or 2 tbsp peanuts), ▢, 1 tablespoon 1 tablespoon (12 grams), urad dal, (skinned black gram), ▢, 7 6 to 8 (12 grams), red chilies, (read notes, I use Byadgi, or use ¾ to 1 tsp mild red chili powder), ▢, ¾, teaspoon teaspoon, cumin seeds, (jeera or sub with shahi jeera for a different flavor), ▢, ¾, teaspoon teaspoon, sea salt, (more to adjust), ▢, ¼, teaspoon teaspoon, turmeric, (optional), ▢, 1 1 ½, teaspoon teaspoon, oil, ▢, ⅓, teaspoon teaspoon, black mustard seeds, ▢, ¼, teaspoon teaspoon, urad dal, (skinned black gram) (optional), ▢, 1 to 2, red chili, ▢, 1, sprig sprig, curry leaves, (pat dry), ▢, 1, pinch, asafoetida, (hing, optional)
instructions: 1. Heat oil in a pan and add chana dal, urad dal & red chilies. Fry them on a medium flame until light golden & aromatic, for 2 to 3 mins. 2. Add cumin seeds, curry leaves, ginger and garlic. Fry them for a minute. Add the onions & saute for 4 to 5 mins, until transparent. 3. Stir in the tomatoes,salt and turmeric. Saute or cook covered, until they break down and turn mushy, for another 5 to 7 mins. If your pan dries out during this process, add a splash of water. 4. Cool down and blend to a smooth or slightly coarse chutney to your liking. (Optionally if you want you may reserve some of the red chilies before blending, to adjust later) Taste test & adjust salt. 5. On a medium flame, heat oil in a small pan. When the oil is medium hot, add mustard, urad dal, red chili and curry leaves, one after the other. Fry until the lentils turn light golden & the curry leaves turn crisp. Turn off and add hing. 6. Pour this over the Tomato Chutney. Serve with South Indian breakfasts like idli, dosa, uttapam etc.

title: Tomato Rice Recipe (Indian Style)
ingredients: ▢, 1 1 ½, cups cups, aged basmati rice, (or any non-sticky rice, refer notes), ▢, 2, tablespoons tablespoons, oil, or ghee, ▢, 1, medium, onion, (chopped or sliced), ▢, 1, green chili, (slit, optional), ▢, 1, tablespoon tablespoon, ginger garlic paste, or tablespoon each fine grated, ▢, 1, cup cup, tomatoes, (2 to 3 medium sized) (chopped or pureed), ▢, ¾, teaspoon teaspoon, salt, (adjust to taste), ▢, 0.75 to 1, teaspoon teaspoon, garam masala, powder, ▢, 0.5 to 1, teaspoon teaspoon, red chili powder, or paprika, ▢, ¼, teaspoon teaspoon, Turmeric, (haldi), ▢, 2 2 ½, cups cups, water for cooker, (3 cups for pot & 1 ¾ cups + 2 tbsp for Instapot), ▢, 3, tablespoon tablespoon, coriander leaves, finely chopped, ▢, 1, tablespoon tablespoon, lemon juice, (optional), ▢, 12, whole cashewnuts, (split & use) (optional), ▢, 1, small, bay leaf, or curry leaves, ▢, ¾, teaspoon teaspoon, cumin seeds, (jeera), ▢, 1, inch inch, cinnamon piece, (dalchini), ▢, 3, green cardamoms, (elaichi), ▢, 3, cloves cloves, (laung)
instructions: 1. Add 1½ cup rice to a bowl and rinse it well a few times until the water runs clear. Pour fresh water and soak the rice for 30 mins. Then drain to a colander & set aside. 2. Heat a pressure cooker or a pot with 2 tbsps ghee or oil. 3. Add cashews and fry until golden. Remove to a plate & set aside. 4. Saute 3 cloves, 1 inch cinnamon piece, ¾ tsp cumin, 3 green cardamoms and 1 small bay leaf. 5. When they begin to crackle, add 1 finely chopped onion and 1 slit green chili. 6. Saute until the onions turn golden. Next saute 1 tbsp ginger garlic paste until a nice aroma comes out for 30 seconds. 7. Next add in 1 cup tomatoes, salt as needed and ¼ tsp turmeric. 8. Saute for 2 to 3 minutes. Cover & cook until the tomatoes break down & turn mushy. 9. Add ½ to 1 tsp red chili powder and ¾ to 1 tsp garam masala powder. 10. Mix and cook until the tomato masala turns completely soft and mushy. 11. At this stage the masala leaves the sides of the pan. Pour water & stir well. Taste the water and add more salt if needed. 12. Bring the water to a rolling boil. Add the drained rice. 13. Cook until the water has almost absorbed. When the rice is still soggy, lower the flame completely and cover with a lid until the tomato rice is cooked completely. 14. Sprinkle chopped coriander leaves and squeeze in some lemon juice. Tomato rice is ready to serve hot or warm with a raita. Garnish with cashews. 15. Stir and cover with the lid. Cook on a medium high heat. Allow to whistle once. When the pressure goes down, open the lid. Fluff up the tomato rice. 16. Soak rice for 30 mins. Press SAUTE button & pour oil to the inner pot of the instant pot. Then fry cashews until golden and remove them. Stir in the whole spices and then add onions. 17. Saute them until transparent. Add the ginger garlic paste & saute for 30 seconds. Add tomatoes and salt. Saute them until the tomatoes break down and turn mushy. 18. Add chilli powder, garam masala & turmeric. Saute for just 1 to 2 mins until 19. the moisture from tomatoes almost dries up. 20. Next pour 1¾ cups + 2 tbsp water. Press CANCEL button. Scrape off any bits of spices stuck at the bottom. This prevents from getting a burn notice. 21. Stir & check the salt. Add more if needed. Then add the rice. Then secure the lid. Set the steam release handle/ value to sealing. Press PRESSURE COOK button (highpressure) for 5 mins. 22. When the tomato rice is done, Instant pot beeps. Wait for 2 mins & release the pressure manually with the help of a spoon by moving the steam release handle to venting from sealing. If you like softer rice then let the pressure release naturally for 7 to 8 mins. Then release the rest manually. 23. Sprinkle coriander leaves & fluff up the tomato rice with a fork. Garnish with cashews.

title: Tomato Soup Recipe
ingredients: ▢, 5 grams 500 grams (1.1 lbs), Tomatoes, cubed, ▢, 1, tablespoon tablespoon, olive oil, or butter, ▢, 1, small, bay leaf, ▢, 1, medium, onion, cubed, ▢, 2, large, garlic, cloves cloves, ▢, ⅓, teaspoon teaspoon, Salt, or as needed, ▢, 1 1 ½, cup cup, water, more if needed, ▢, 1, tablespoon tablespoon, sugar, ▢, ¾, tablespoon tablespoon, corn starch, (corn flour or arrowroot powder or 1 small boiled potato), ▢, ½, teaspoon teaspoon, pepper, or as desired, ▢, 0.75 to 1, teaspoon teaspoon, herbs, (I use mixed Italian herbs), ▢, 2, stalks, Basil, (optional), ▢, 2, tablespoon tablespoon, cream, (or 25 cashew nuts), ▢, 1, small, carrot, (optional) (diced)
instructions: 1. Heat oil in a pan. Add bay leaf, garlic and onions. Saute all of these on a high flame for 3 to 4 mins. 2. Then add in tomatoes and salt. Stir and cook until soft. 3. Next add in basil and cook until tomatoes turn completely mushy. Turn off and cool. 4. Discard the basil & bay leaf. 5. Add the mushy tomato mixture along with the juices to a blender jar. 6. Pour 1 cup water or stock and blend smooth. 7. Place a sieve over a pot & transfer the blended tomato soup to it. 8. Filter and discard the leftovers. Bring it to a boil on a medium heat & simmer for 2 to 3 mins. 9. Meanwhile stir in corn starch in half cup water and add it gradually to the boiling tomato soup. 10. Stir constantly and boil until slightly thick. 11. Add sugar & more salt if desired. Simmer until it reaches a desired consistency. 12. Next add herbs & pepper. Cover the soup & turn off the stove. 13. Allow it to cool down a bit before adding the cream. If you prefer to use cashew nuts, soak them in ¼ cup hot water for 30 minutes. Add them to a small chutney grinder along with the soaked water and puree until you get a thick and smooth cream. If needed add a few tablespoons more water for consistency. Use this to top your tomato soup while serving. 14. Transfer tomato soup to serving bowls. Garnish with herbs & cream. Enjoy with croutons. 15. Spread butter on the bread slices and toast them on a medium heat until crisp. 16. Then cut them to bite sized pieces.

title: Upma Recipe
ingredients: ▢, 1, cup cup, Rava, (Bombay rava/ sooji/semolina), ▢, 2 2 to 2 ½, tablespoons tablespoons, oil, or ghee, ▢, ½, teaspoon teaspoon, mustard seeds, ▢, ½, teaspoon teaspoon, cumin seeds, (jeera, optional, adds aroma), ▢, 1, teaspoon teaspoon, split urad dal, (skinned split black gram), ▢, 1 1 ½, teaspoon teaspoon, chana dal, (optional, adds nice aroma, Bengal gram), ▢, 2, tablespoons tablespoons, peanuts, (optional), ▢, 8 to 12, cashews, (you may leave out if using peanuts), ▢, 1, sprig sprig, curry leaves, ▢, 1, pinch, asafoetida, (hing) (optional), ▢, ¼, cup cup, onion, (chopped or sliced thinly), ▢, 1 to 2, green chili, slit, ▢, ½, inch inch, ginger, (½ to ¾ teaspoon minced or grated)(optional), ▢, 3, cups cups, water, (2 ¾ cups for a drier upma), ▢, 0.5 to, teaspoon teaspoon, salt, ▢, 2, teaspoon teaspoon, ghee, (optional), ▢, lemon juice, to serve, ▢, ½, cup cup, mixed vegetables, (fine chopped carrots, beans and green peas)
instructions: 1. Roast rava: 2. On a medium heat, dry roast rava in a pan, stirring often until crunchy and aromatic. Do not brown it. Remove to a bowl and keep aside to cool down. 3. Optional – Roast peanuts to garnish 4. – Fry peanuts in 1 teaspoon oil until golden, aromatic and crunchy. Reserve aside to garnish. 5. Soak dal 6. – If you like your dal softer, you may soak them in ¼ cup hot water for 10 mins, drain & pat dry before using. 7. Temper the spices: 8. Pour oil to the hot pan & add mustard seeds, cumin seeds, urad dal & chana dal. Fry until the dal turns light golden. Add cashews and fry until they turn golden & aromatic. Stir in hing, green chilies, ginger and curry leaves. 9. Saute for a minute & add chopped onions. Saute until transparent & add mix vegetables (optional) if using. Stir and cook covered until slightly tender. 10. Boil water: 11. Pour water and add salt. Mix and taste test the water. It has to be slightly salty. If required add more and bring the water to a rolling boil. 12. Make upma: 13. Lower the heat. With one hand pour rava in a slow stream to the boiling water, while you incorporate it into the water with the other hand. (check video). Be quick at this stage. Stir everything once to check any lumps. Break up if any. 14. Cover and cook on the lowest flame until the water is completely absorbed. This just takes 2 to 4 mins depending on the kind of pot or pan used. Add ghee and give a quick stir. Turn off and cover back to rest for 5 mins. 15. Stir in the lemon juice and serve rava upma plain or with spiced 16. peanut podi 17. , chutney, pickle or curd. Garnish with roasted/fried peanuts.

title: Veg Cutlet Recipe (Vegetable Cutlet)
ingredients: ▢, 2, cups cups, potatoes, peel cube to inch, ▢, ½, cup cup, green peas, (frozen or fresh), ▢, 1, cup cup, carrot, diced, ▢, ½, cup cup, beans, chopped, ▢, 3, tablespoon tablespoon, coriander leaves, or cilantro finely chopped, ▢, ½, teaspoon teaspoon, salt, (adjust to taste), ▢, ¾, teaspoon teaspoon, ginger grated, or paste, ▢, 1 to 2, green chilies, finely chopped, ▢, 0.5 to, teaspoon teaspoon, garam masala, ▢, 6, tablespoon tablespoon, bread crumbs, or cup thick poha powdered, ▢, 4, tablespoons tablespoons, oil, or as needed, ▢, 2, tablespoon tablespoon, besan, or gram flour, ▢, ½, cup cup, bread crumbs, or powdered poha for the crust
instructions: 1. Peel and chop potatoes to ¾ inch cubes, carrots to ½ inch and beans to smaller bits. 2. Steamer/pot 3. – Transfer all the chopped vegetables & peas to a steamer basket and steam them until fork tender and not mushy or water logged. 4. or Pressure cook 5. – I pressure cook all the vegetables together for 2 whistles on a medium heat with half cup water. Drain any excess moisture left and use up in your soups. 6. Meanwhile, If you do not have bread crumbs, add some poha to a mixer jar and make a super fine powder. 7. Add the boiled vegetables to a mixing bowl, cool down slightly and mash them well with a masher. 8. Add bread crumbs, salt, grated ginger, garam masala, coriander leaves and chilies. Mix well to form a non-sticky dough. 9. If using powdered poha, start with 2 tablespoon only 10. and mix well. If it is still too sticky, add more crumbs or powder only as required. 11. (See photos in the post for texture) 12. Taste test and add more salt or spices to your taste. Divide the cutlet dough to 13 to 14 parts. Roll to balls. 13. Flatten them gently and make thick patties. Keep them aside in a plate and cover to prevent drying. 14. In another plate add ¼ cup bread crumbs or powdered poha for the crust. To another mixing bowl, add besan or rice flour. Pour water just enough to make a slightly thin/runny batter. For a thicker crust, make a thicker batter 15. Arrange all the 3 – Cutlets plate, batter bowl & bread crumbs plate side by side. 16. Dip each vegetable cutlet in the batter to coat on both the sides. Then place them on the bread crumbs. Gently coat them with the crumbs all over. 17. Place them back on a plate aside. Repeat the same steps of dipping them in the batter and then the crumbs for all the veg cutlets. Leave this aside for about 5 to 10 minutes. 18. Just before frying, gently shake them off in the plate to remove the excess crumbs. 19. Lastly heat 1 tablespoon oil for shallow frying in a wide pan. When the oil turns hot, gently place the veg cutlet in the pan. 20. Fry them on both the sides until golden and crisp. Set aside and fry them in 2 batches using more oil as needed. 21. To bake the veg cutlet – 22. Preheat the oven to 180 C/ 360 C for 15 mins and place the patties on a greased tray. Bake for about 15 mins on each side or until golden and crisp. 23. Serve veg cutlet with green chutney or any sauce.

title: Veg Fried Rice Recipe (Vegetable Fried Rice)
ingredients: ▢, 1, cup cup, Rice, (uncooked or 3 cups cooked like basmati, jasmine), ▢, 1, tablespoon tablespoon, Garlic, chopped, ▢, 2, tablespoons tablespoons, oil, (peanut, sunflower or avocado), ▢, 1, tablespoon tablespoon, Soya sauce, (or tamari, adjust to taste), ▢, 1, teaspoon teaspoon, Vinegar, (rice vinegar or apple cider), ▢, Salt, as needed, ▢, ½, teaspoon teaspoon, Black pepper, coarsely crushed, ▢, 0.25 to, cup cup, Carrots, finely, ▢, ¼, cup cup, Bell peppers, finely, ▢, ¼, cup cup, French beans, (4 to 5 green beans, chopped finely), ▢, ¼, cup cup, Green onions, (scallions/spring onions, 4 stalks, fine chopped), ▢, ¼, cup cup, Cabbage, (optional, or ¾ cup mushrooms sliced ), ▢, 1, green chili, slit
instructions: 1. Skip this section if you have precooked rice. Wash rice a few times till the water runs clear. 2. Soak it for at least 10 mins. Meanwhile bring 4 to 5 cups water to a boil in a large pot. 3. When the water begins to boil rapidly, drain the rice and add it to the water. Pour 1 tsp oil to prevent the rice from turning sticky. 4. Cook rice till al dente. Rice must not be mushy but has to be grainy. 5. Drain off the rice to a colander. Cool it completely & fluff it up with a fork. 6. While the rice cools, chop the veggies and garlic. Keep aside 2 tablespoons of green onions for garnish. 7. Heat a wok with oil on the highest flame and fry chopped garlic slightly just for 30 to 40 seconds. 8. Next add all the chopped vegetables and fry for 2 to 3 minutes just until half cooked. They should be crunchy and not soft. 9. Add soya sauce. Then add the optional ingredients vinegar and half tsp sugar. Sugar helps to preserve the bright colors of veggies and helps to balance the flavors. 10. Immediately add cooled rice, ¼ teaspoon salt and black pepper. Fry the vegetable fried rice for 2 mins. 11. Garnish veg fried rice with fresh green onions. Tastes good on its own but you can also pair it with veg manchurian or chilli paneer.

title: Veg Puff (Bakery Vegetable Puffs)
ingredients: ▢, 1, puff pastry sheet, (cut to 4 squares), ▢, 1, tablespoon tablespoon, oil, (15 ml), ▢, ½, teaspoon teaspoon, crushed fennel seeds, (optional), ▢, 1, small, onion, (⅓ cup/45 grams fine chopped, may omit), ▢, 1, sprig sprig, curry leaves, (optional), ▢, 1, green chili, (chopped, optional), ▢, 1, teaspoon teaspoon, ginger garlic paste, (or ¾ tsp each grated), ▢, 0.25 to, teaspoon teaspoon, Kashmiri red chili powder, or paprika, ▢, ¼, teaspoon teaspoon, turmeric, ▢, ½, teaspoon teaspoon, garam masala, (more if you want), ▢, ½, teaspoon teaspoon, sea salt, (adjust to taste), ▢, 1, teaspoon teaspoon, lemon juice, (optional), ▢, 1, tablespoon tablespoon, coriander leaves, (or 1 tsp kasuri methi), ▢, 1 cup 1 cup, diced potato, (150 grams - 1 medium, peel & dice to ¾ inch), ▢, 0.5 cup ½ cup, diced carrots, (70 grams - 1 medium, peel & dice), ▢, 0.5 cup ½ cup, green peas, (60 grams - frozen or fresh, may sub half with sweet corn), ▢, 2, tablespoons tablespoons, unsalted melted butter, (milk or a beaten egg with 2 tbsps milk)
instructions: 1. Place diced potatoes, carrots & peas in a steaming basket or a wide bowl. Steam in a pot or pressure cooker until cooked through. I use my instant pot to steam on high pressure for 6 mins. 2. Heat a pan with oil and add the fennel seeds. When they begin to sizzle add onions, curry leaves, green chilies and half of the salt. Saute until light golden for 4 mins. 3. Add ginger garlic and fry for a minute. Reduce the flame and stir in chili powder, turmeric, garam masala and remaining salt. 4. Pour 2 to 3 tablespoon of water and deglaze the pan. Stir in the steamed veggies & saute for 2 mins. Turn off. 5. Mix in coriander leaves and lemon juice if you want. Taste test to adjust salt. Cool down completely. If you want may remove the curry leaves & discard. 6. Meanwhile thaw your puff pastry sheets at room temperature, until easy enough to handle. Mine takes only 5 to 6 mins. (Depends on your indoor temp) 7. Cut each pastry sheet to 4 portions. Place 3 to 4 tbsps vegetable stuffing in the center of each cut puff pastry. (don't overfill) Brush 2 parallel edges with little water. 8. Bring one edge over the other and gently tap with your fingers, don’t press down with pressure. I prefer to leave a little gap on the sides for the steam to escape. This keeps the puffs crispy slightly longer. 9. Brush with unsalted melted butter or milk and place in the refrigerator for 15 to 20 mins. Meanwhile preheat the oven to 400 F/ 200 C for 15 to 20 mins. 10. Place in the center and bake for 20 to 22 mins or as instructed on the package, until golden & flaky. (Smaller ovens and toasters may take only 15 mins) 11. Cool the tray on a wired rack for at least 5 mins. Serve Vegetable puffs hot or warm with ketchup, cilantro/coriander chutney & chai.
